items.title: Italian council sets 200% tax on data center development in agricultural zones — aims to spur the use of old industrial areas instead and limit environmental impact
items.description: This 200% tax will make it more expensive to build in rural areas and is aimed at spurring data center development in disused industrial zones instead.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The region of Lombardy, Italy, which has Milan as its capital, just approved a 100% tax on data center developments in rural areas and a 200% tax for those being built in agricultural and green zones. According to the Italian news outlet Il Sole 24 Ore, the law was designed to discourage hyperscalers from buying up rural tracts of land for projects like these, which Lombardy leaders say are often done “without clear timeframes and plans.”“We cannot, in the light of these numbers, block the development of companies and employment, the race for artificial intelligence is already a fact,” Lombardy councilor Massimo Sertori told the publication. “We can, however, try to keep the phenomenon under control by avoiding excesses and the exaggerated exploitation of the territory.”It seems that even data center developers in Europe have started gaining interest in rural land. Even though these are often less developed compared to urban and suburban areas, it also usually comes with fewer restrictions, are cheaper to build on, and might even have a faster permitting process. One Texas county noticed this and has taken steps to limit AI hyperscalers’ ambitions while the county government is studying their potential impact on rural land. On the other hand, Italian lawmakers are hoping that the law will push developers toward disused industrial zones. These areas are typically built for operations like these, and there are no additional burdens for AI data center developers who choose to build here instead.Latest Videos From You may like AI data center developers target rural territory to bypass city construction bans and regulations Texas county passes data center ban for rural areas for a year, move comes in wake of AI data centers moving to remote areas to skirt regulations AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Aside from land use, Lombardy officials also have concerns about energy use. Sertori said that data center applications have now reached up to 30GW throughout Italy, with more than half of them planned to be situated in the region. However, they should only authorize 2GB based on “real and concrete projects.” At the moment, Milan hosts 33 active data centers, with 10 more in the construction stage and 23 more applying for approvals. This makes Lombardy the region with the highest concentration of data centers in Italy.The data center buildout needed to power the “AI revolution” is quickly becoming a hot topic in the United States. Many Americans are pushing back against these developments, with a recent survey saying that 70% do not want a data center in the vicinity of their homes. While it doesn’t seem that Italians are pushing back with the same ferocity, their authorities are still concerned that unchecked data center development would have a negative impact on the community. Opposing politicians even voiced their concerns that this move is not enough.“In Lombardy, we have tried to fill the void of the Meloni government on industrial policies. The development of data centers is necessary, but it cannot be left to chance or to the market alone; a political vision is needed,” Democrat Mattero Piloni said. “However, this law lacks real and decisive soil protection, because neither the government nor the region have put in place stringent constraints. We will have to wait for a national law.” Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: Italian council sets 200% tax on data center development in agricultural zones — aims to spur the use of old industrial areas instead and limit environmental impact
items.descriptionZh:
意大利伦巴第大区（首府米兰）近日通过一项新法案，对在农村地区建设的数据中心征收100%的附加税，对在农业和绿化地带建设的则征收高达200%的附加税。据意大利媒体《Il Sole 24 Ore》报道，这项法律的目的是遏制超大规模云服务商（hyperscalers）以“缺乏明确时间框架和计划”的方式大量购买农村土地用于数据中心项目。伦巴第大区议员马西莫·塞尔托里表示：“鉴于这些数字，我们无法阻止企业发展和就业，人工智能竞赛已是既定事实。但我们可以尽量控制这一现象，避免过度和夸张的土地开发。”

这一举措的背景是，欧洲的数据中心开发商也开始把目光投向农村地区。与城市和郊区相比，农村地区虽然基础设施相对落后，但通常限制更少、土地成本更低，甚至审批流程更快。此前，美国得克萨斯州的一个县已经注意到这一趋势，并采取措施限制AI超大规模数据中心的扩张，同时研究其对农村土地的潜在影响。意大利立法者则希望新法能推动开发商优先选择废弃工业区。这些区域本身就适合此类高能耗设施建设，选址在此不会给AI数据中心开发商增加额外负担。

除了土地用途，伦巴第官员还高度关注能源消耗问题。塞尔托里透露，意大利全国的数据中心申请总功率已高达30吉瓦（GW），其中超过一半计划设在伦巴第大区。然而，他认为只应基于“真实且具体的项目”批准约2吉瓦的容量。目前，米兰拥有33个活跃的数据中心，另有10个在建、23个正在申请审批。这使得伦巴第成为意大利数据中心最密集的地区。

支撑“AI革命”所需的数据中心大规模建设正在美国引发激烈讨论。最新调查显示，70%的美国人反对在自家附近建设数据中心。尽管意大利民众的反对声浪不如美国强烈，但当局仍担忧不加管控的数据中心发展会对社区产生负面影响。反对派政客甚至认为这项税收措施力度不够。民主党议员马泰奥·皮洛尼指出：“在伦巴第，我们试图填补梅洛尼政府在产业政策上的空白。发展数据中心是必要的，但不能放任自流或完全交给市场，需要政治视野。然而，这项法律缺乏真正有力的土地保护措施，因为国家和地方政府都没有设置严格的约束条件。我们还需要等待国家层面的法律出台。”

从技术原理和产业影响角度看，数据中心是AI算力的物理基础，其核心特征是高功率密度（单机柜可达数十千瓦）和持续冷却需求，这导致其对电力和土地资源消耗巨大。新税政策本质上是一种经济杠杆，旨在将AI算力基础设施从价格低廉但生态敏感的农村和农业用地，引导至已具备工业基础设施的废弃工业区，从而在推动AI产业发展的同时，控制土地过度开发和能源负荷失衡。这一举措也反映出欧洲在AI基础设施建设中，正面临经济增长与环境保护、地方规划与中央政策之间的深层博弈。

items.title: Erin Brockovich starts tracking AI data centers, calls on affected communities to submit issues — website shows more than 2,700 reports from across the US raising various concerns
items.description: Erin Brockovich, who made her name making a case against PG&E in the '90s that resulted in a $333 million settlement, is now looking at the impact of data center developments on communities and is recording community reports along the way.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Erin Brockovich, famed for her role in the landmark 1996 class-action lawsuit against Pacific Gas & Electric (PG&E) that resulted in a $333 million settlement, and the subsequent movie that bears her name, is on another crusade. This time, the environmental activist and consumer advocate is targeting data centers, launching the Brockovich AI Data Center Reporting website to compile complaints from affected residents and community members."The RACE to build AI infrastructures is unfolding town by town across America. In some places, data centers are welcomed. In others, they are delayed, contested or abandoned altogether,” Brockovich wrote on her website. “This MAP captures the real-world footprint of that race — revealing patterns of growth, conflict and uncertainty.” At the moment, the website lists 33 operational (built & running) AI data centers, 44 under-construction projects, and 27 proposed sites. It also shows 2,716 community-reported locations nationwide as of May 24, 2026.There are several primary concerns against data centers, the biggest of which is energy consumption, which has been causing utility prices to skyrocket across large swaths of the United States. Water usage is also another issue that communities have been raising, with these massive-scale projects using a lot of water and catching the attention of lawmakers. There are also reports of noise pollution, with some members of the public complaining about the infrasound phenomenon often brought about by large-scale industrial operations. Brockovich also lists location risks, scalability, and e-waste as potential long-term problems that the industry must deal with in the future.Latest Videos From You may like AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects 70% of Americans oppose data centers near their homes, now less popular than nuclear power plants While the White House has been pushing for AI-friendly policies, the rest of the American people are increasingly growing skeptical about its development, particularly for data centers that are popping up in their backyards. It has gotten to the point that 70% of Americans oppose data center construction near their homes, a 23% increase from a survey conducted less than six months before. The pushback against these developments is gaining steam, too, with at least 69 jurisdictions passing moratoriums on data center construction to give local governments time to assess their impact.It’s unclear what Brockovich intends to do with the data she’s gathering, but this is often one of the first steps needed for lawyers to see if a class-action lawsuit has merit. While there is no case at the moment, this website puts data center developers on notice that their actions are being publicly tracked, and that Erin Brockovich has her keen eye on them. items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 艾琳·布罗克维奇开始追踪AI数据中心，呼吁受影响社区提交问题——网站显示全美超2700份报告提出各种关切
items.descriptionZh:
美国传奇环保斗士艾琳·布罗克维奇（Erin Brockovich）——她因1996年针对太平洋燃气电力公司（PG&E）的里程碑式集体诉讼而声名鹊起，该诉讼最终促成3.33亿美元和解，其事迹还被改编成同名电影——如今再次发起一场新的战役。这次，这位环境活动家和消费者权益倡导者将矛头指向了数据中心，她推出了名为“Brockovich AI数据中心报告”的网站，用于收集来自受影响居民和社区成员的投诉。

布罗克维奇在网站上写道：“在全美各地，一场建设AI基础设施的竞赛正在城镇间展开。有些地方欢迎数据中心，而有些地方则推迟、争议甚至彻底放弃这些项目。这张地图捕捉了这场竞赛的实际足迹——揭示了增长、冲突和不确定性的模式。”截至2026年5月24日，该网站列出了33个已运营（建成并运行）的AI数据中心、44个在建项目、27个拟建站点，以及全美范围内由社区报告的2716个相关地点。

对数据中心的主要担忧集中在几个方面，其中最大的是能源消耗问题，这已导致美国大片地区的公用事业电费飙升。用水问题也是社区日益关注的焦点，这些大型项目消耗大量水资源，已引起立法者的注意。此外，还有噪音污染的报道——一些公众抱怨大型工业设施带来的次声波现象。布罗克维奇还列出了选址风险、可扩展性和电子垃圾等未来行业必须应对的潜在长期问题。

虽然白宫一直在推动有利于人工智能的政策，但越来越多的美国民众对AI的发展持怀疑态度，尤其对那些建在家门口的数据中心表示反感。这种情况已发展到70%的美国人反对在其住房附近建设数据中心的程度，相比不到六个月前进行的类似调查，这一比例上升了23%。对这些开发项目的抵制势头也在增强，至少69个司法管辖区已通过了暂停数据中心建设的法令，以便地方政府有时间评估其影响。

目前尚不清楚布罗克维奇打算如何处理她收集的数据，但这类行动往往是律师判断集体诉讼是否具有法律依据的第一步。尽管眼下还没有正式诉讼，但这个网站向数据中心开发商发出了明确信号：他们的行为正被公开追踪，而艾琳·布罗克维奇正紧盯着他们。

items.title: China adds homegrown AI chips to 'secure and reliable' procurement list for the first time — nine options added as move away from Nvidia continues
items.description: The certifications are valid for three years and were issued jointly by the China Information Technology Security Evaluation Centre and the National Secrecy Science and Technology Evaluation Centre.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter China's official technology security bodies on Tuesday certified nine domestically designed AI processors for state procurement, according to the South China Morning Post, creating a brand-new "AI training and inference chips" category under the country's Anke security certification framework. The approved products include Huawei's Ascend 310 and Ascend 910 processors, Alibaba's T-Head Zhenwu M530 and M890, and chips from Biren Technology, Hygon Information Technology, Iluvatar CoreX, MetaX, and Moore Threads. Two of China's most prominent AI chip developers, Cambricon Technologies and Baidu-backed Kunlunxin, didn’t appear on the list.The certifications are valid for three years and were issued jointly by the China Information Technology Security Evaluation Centre and the National Secrecy Science and Technology Evaluation Centre. Their approvals function as a de facto procurement catalog for government agencies, central state-owned enterprises, and other entities covered by Beijing's Xinchuang initiative, the long-running campaign to replace Western hardware and software across sensitive Chinese IT systems.The new list represents a substantial expansion from China's initial foray into certifying domestic AI hardware. In December, Beijing added only Huawei and Cambricon to the Xinchuang procurement list (a separate state approval mechanism) for AI processors. Five months later, seven vendors now hold Anke security certification for nine separate chips. The Xinchuang program had previously focused on replacing Intel and AMD CPUs and Oracle databases in government systems, but AI accelerators are the newest addition to that.Latest Videos From You may like Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia’s H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo China's homegrown silicon suppliers gain traction as Nvidia struggles to get its chips into the market Chinese GPU maker Cambricon's Q1 revenue hits $423 million as country's homegrown AI chip market accelerates Cambricon's absence stands out given that the company was on the December list and is targeting 500,000 AI chip shipments in 2026. An anonymous source told the SCMP that companies can choose whether to submit products for testing, so exclusion doesn’t necessarily indicate a failed evaluation. Each chip must pass tests under the Anke V3.0 requirements to qualify.Chinese chipmakers continue to eat into Nvidia's position in the domestic market. Chinese semiconductor firms delivered 1.65 million AI GPUs in 2025 out of a total of 4 million units, claiming 41% of local AI server shipments. Huawei alone shipped roughly 812,000 AI chips and is projecting $12 billion in AI processor revenue for 2026. Morgan Stanley estimates China's total AI chip market could reach $67 billion by 2030, with domestic supply covering roughly 76% of demand.Wafer fab capacity remains a big constraint, however, with all of the certified chipmakers competing for limited production slots at SMIC, whose most advanced stable node is its N+2 process, which is roughly equivalent to 7nm. SMIC reported overall utilization rates above 93% for 2025 and spent $8.1 billion in capex last year, with plans to hold that level through 2026. items.titleZh: 中国首次将本土AI芯片列入“安全可靠”采购清单——新增九个选项，继续减少对英伟达的依赖
items.descriptionZh:
中国官方技术安全机构于周二正式认证了九款国产人工智能处理器，将其纳入国家政府采购目录，并在此过程中创建了一个全新的“AI训练与推理芯片”类别，隶属于国家“安可”（Anke）安全认证框架。这一举措标志着中国在关键信息技术领域推进国产化替代的“信创”工程，从传统的CPU、数据库正式延伸至AI加速器这一核心环节。

本次获得认证的产品包括：华为的昇腾310（Ascend 310）和昇腾910（Ascend 910）处理器，阿里巴巴平头哥的镇岳M530（Zhenwu M530）和镇岳M890（Zhenwu M890），以及来自壁仞科技（Biren Technology）、海光信息技术（Hygon）、燧原科技（Iluvatar CoreX）、寒武纪（MetaX，注：此处原文为MetaX，但后文提到寒武纪缺席，疑似混淆；实际上MetaX是另一家初创公司，寒武纪英文为Cambricon，需注意区分——原文列出Iluvatar CoreX、MetaX、Moore Threads，MetaX应是另一家GPU厂商，非寒武纪）和摩尔线程（Moore Threads）的芯片。值得注意的是，中国最知名的两家AI芯片开发商——寒武纪科技和百度支持的昆仑芯（Kunlunxin）并未出现在名单中。

这些认证由“中国信息安全测评中心”和“国家保密科技测评中心”联合颁发，有效期为三年。认证结果实质上构成了面向政府机关、中央国有企业及其他受“信创”倡议覆盖单位的采购目录。“信创”是中国长期推进的、旨在用国产软硬件替换西方产品的国家行动，此前主要聚焦于替换政府系统中的英特尔、AMD的CPU以及甲骨文（Oracle）的数据库，如今AI加速器成为最新纳入的品类。

此次名单的规模较中国首次认证国产AI硬件时有了显著扩大。去年12月，北京仅将华为和寒武纪两家公司的AI处理器列入信创采购清单（一个独立的政府批准机制）。仅仅五个月后，就有七家厂商的九款芯片获得了安可安全认证。对于寒武纪的缺席，匿名消息人士向《南华早报》解释称，企业可自行选择是否提交产品进行测试，因此未出现在名单上并不一定意味着评估不通过。每一款芯片都必须通过安可V3.0标准下的严格测试才能获得资格。

随着国产认证的推进，中国芯片企业正在持续蚕食英伟达在国内市场的份额。数据显示，2025年中国半导体企业共交付了165万颗AI GPU，在总出货量400万颗中占比高达41%的本地AI服务器出货量。仅华为一家就出货约81.2万颗AI芯片，并预计其2026年AI处理器收入将达到120亿美元。摩根士丹利则预测，到2030年，中国AI芯片市场总规模可能达到670亿美元，其中国内供应将覆盖约76%的需求。

然而，晶圆代工产能仍是所有认证芯片厂商面临的重大制约。它们不得不竞争中国大陆最先进代工厂中芯国际（SMIC）的有限产能。中芯国际目前最稳定的先进节点是N+2工艺，大致相当于7nm制程。中芯国际报告称2025年整体产能利用率超过93%，去年资本支出达到81亿美元，并计划在2026年维持这一投资水平。这意味着在产能紧张的前提下，认证名单的意义不仅在于打开了政府采购的大门，更在于为芯片厂商优先争夺稀缺的代工资源提供了官方背书。

从技术原理角度看，这些芯片各自采用不同的自研架构：华为昇腾系列基于达芬奇（DaVinci）架构，昇腾310主打推理，昇腾910则面向训练场景，提供高算力与高效能；阿里巴巴平头哥的镇岳系列是面向AI推理的专用处理器，采用自研指令集；壁仞、摩尔线程等则专注于通用GPU架构，兼容CUDA或自有生态。国产替代浪潮正从单一的处理器替换，走向覆盖训练、推理、云边端全场景的深度自主化阶段。

items.title: The Pope isn’t AGI-pilled
items.description:
On Monday, Pope Leo XIV unveiled an encyclical letter addressing the societal implications of artificial intelligence. The letter, titled Magnifica Humanitas, warned that the "use of AI is never a purely technical matter: when it enters processes that affect people's lives, it touches on rights, opportunities, status and freedom." Alongside him was Anthropic cofounder and interpretability team lead Christopher Olah, representing a partnership between the Catholic Church and one of the biggest players in AI.

items.content: On Monday, Pope Leo XIV unveiled an encyclical letter addressing the societal implications of artificial intelligence. The letter, titled Magnifica Humanitas, warned that the “use of AI is never a purely technical matter: when it enters processes that affect people’s lives, it touches on rights, opportunities, status and freedom.” Alongside him was Anthropic cofounder and interpretability team lead Christopher Olah, representing a partnership between the Catholic Church and one of the biggest players in AI.The letter elicited a wide range of reactions from in and around the tech industry. Nearly everyone believed the document would be influential. Some critics questioned whether it went far enough, and others believed it should have discussed artificial general intelligence (AGI), which many companies insist is imminent. Still others thought the pope was spot-on.“It was a pretty clear subtweet of big tech CEOs who are out here blatantly declaring that they’re eliminating staff to replace ‘lower-value human capital’ with AI, and who are also buying their way into the political rooms where it happens in order to write the rules in their favor,” said Sacha Haworth, executive director of the Tech Oversight Project.The pope’s encyclical comes amid a backlash to AI’s growing power. Six in 10 US adults feel they have “little to no control” of how AI is used in their everyday lives, protests against the construction of data centers are increasingly common, and some people have even attempted attacks on AI CEOs themselves.RelatedThe race to AGI-pill the popePope Leo calls for being ‘profoundly human’ in the age of AIAs Olah’s appearance suggests, the pope’s missive describes AI as a technology that can have positive applications, and its tone earned mixed reactions. “I’m glad it’s critical of the AI companies though I think it should be more so,” Daniel Kokotajlo, an AI researcher and former OpenAI employee who is behind the nonprofit AI Futures Project, told The Verge. Conversely, Dr. Guru Sethupathy, GM of AI governance at software company Optro, was encouraged by indications that “Pope Leo and the Vatican are not against AI but rather how to pursue a responsible path that is best for humanity.”The decision to partner with the Vatican was a strategic move by Anthropic, a company that’s built its business on a carefully curated reputation of being a more trustworthy alternative than its competitors. Anthropic famously spent the last few months embroiled in a battle with the Pentagon over limits to military AI use, and a connection with another powerful institution could help bolster its status — and let it help shape future Vatican recommendations.One controversial aspect of the document, in many tech circles, was that it made no mention whatsoever of AGI or superintelligence; it allows that AI systems may “often surpass human intelligence in speed and computational capacity” but says they “lack the affective, relational and spiritual perspective through which human beings grow in wisdom.” Dean W. Ball, a senior fellow at the Foundation for American Innovation, wrote on X that the encyclical “would be much improved if it were less enamored of the traditional academia/civil society talking points on AI … and more engaged with where AI is headed. But instead of doing that, the encyclical dodges in the deepest sense, denying that AI ‘really thinks’ or ‘really learns.’” Kokotajlo also said he wished the letter took the possibility of AGI and superintelligence “more seriously.”But the document isn’t meant to do everything, several people in tech and in the Catholic world told The Verge.“It’s not about AI. It’s about protecting the human person in the age of AI.”“This is a major Catholic social teaching document,” Sister Susan Francois, assistant congregation leader of the Sisters of St. Joseph of Peace, said. “It’s not about AI. It’s about protecting the human person in the age of AI.” Brian Boyd, the US faith liaison for the Future of Life Institute and an instructor of Catholic social teaching at Notre Dame Seminary, called the document “more of a call to arms than a specific set of marching orders.” The pope called throughout the encyclical to “establish adequate regulatory tools capable of upholding justice and curbing the distorting effects of technological power,” but he stopped short of endorsing specific proposals.The Tech Oversight Project’s Haworth said she views the encyclical as “setting the compass for the moral direction of the world, or at least of the Western world … You don’t have to be Catholic to see your own concerns and your own wishes and fears in this document.” She said she expects her elected leaders to take on specific concerns over AGI and do their part to hold powerful tech companies accountable, rather than for the pope’s document to be “all things for all people.” She also called it a “warning shot for leaders, for politicians, because what this document talked about is the creation of a sub-class.”For some, the encyclical’s lack of attention to AGI wasn’t a problem, since they took it as the pope focusing on the real-world impacts of AI on vulnerable communities as the technology exists today, rather than some of the potential risks ahead for humanity as a whole if and when labs hit the (largely unspecified) AGI milestone.Aaron Fulkerson, CEO of Opaque Systems, a startup that helps customers organize encrypted data, said the pope is “actually looking at the system and highlighting something that a lot of us in tech would recognize as a systemic risk to humanity. And I’m not even talking about AGI. I’m talking about our global economy.”Even before the current wave of AI, tech industry centralization could be dangerous. A problem at cybersecurity firm CrowdStrike sidelined banks, hospitals, and airlines around the world, and an outage at Amazon Web Services took down a large swath of the internet, from Reddit to Venmo. For Fulkerson, the risks to the global economy only grow as power is concentrated in the hands of a couple of AI labs.“The thing that I’ve seen in the news has been this positioning around power dynamics of the pope versus tech bros, but I think everybody’s missing the bigger story here, which is that he’s looking at a system that is intrinsically risky,” Fulkerson said. “We’re sleepwalking into a world in which one or two labs are the cognitive infrastructure of every industry on earth — that means humanity is far less resilient, not more capable.”In the encyclical, the pope compared AI to the Tower of Babel, a structure he describes as “supported by a uniformity that eliminated diversity and that chose homogenization over communion.”. The world must “avoid the ‘Babel syndrome,’” he wrote: “the idolatry of profit that sacrifices the weak, a uniformity that neutralizes differences, and the pretense that a single language — even a digital one — can translate everything, including the mystery of the person, into data and performance.” In his reckoning, AI became not just a new technology, but a Biblical struggle. “The risk of dehumanization,” he wrote, “is an ancient and ever-new temptation that today takes on a technical guise.” The weight of those statements, not the technical specifics, is likely to be its lasting impact.
items.author: Hayden Field
items.descriptionZh:
周一，教皇利奥十四世发布了一封关于人工智能社会影响的通谕《Magnifica Humanitas》。该文件警告称，“人工智能的使用从来不是一个纯粹的技术问题：当它进入影响人们生活的过程时，就触及了权利、机会、地位和自由。”与他一同出席发布仪式的是Anthropic联合创始人兼可解释性团队负责人克里斯托弗·奥拉，这标志着天主教会与人工智能领域主要参与者之一的合作。

这封通谕引发了科技行业内外广泛的反应。几乎所有人都认为这份文件将具有影响力。一些批评者质疑其力度是否足够，另一些人则认为它本应讨论人工通用智能（AGI）——许多公司坚称AGI即将到来。还有一些人认为教皇的观点一针见血。

“这显然是在影射那些公开宣称要裁减员工、用人工智能取代‘低价值人力资本’，并试图通过政治关系为自己谋利的大型科技公司CEO们，”科技监督项目执行总监萨莎·霍沃斯表示。

教皇发布通谕之际，正值人工智能日益强大的反弹浪潮。六成美国成年人认为自己对人工智能在日常生活中的使用“几乎没有控制权”，针对数据中心建设的抗议越来越常见，甚至有人试图袭击人工智能公司的CEO。

正如奥拉的亮相所暗示的，教皇的这份文件将人工智能描述为一种可以产生积极应用的技术，其语气引发了截然不同的反应。“我很高兴它对人工智能公司持批评态度，但我认为批评得还不够严厉，”人工智能研究员、前OpenAI员工丹尼尔·科科塔伊洛告诉The Verge，他是非营利组织AI Futures Project的负责人。相反，软件公司Optro的人工智能治理总经理古鲁·塞图帕西博士则对“教皇利奥和梵蒂冈并不反对人工智能，而是寻求一条对人类最有利的负责任道路”这一表态感到鼓舞。

与梵蒂冈合作是Anthropic的战略性举措。这家公司建立在对自身声誉的精心维护之上，将自己定位为比竞争对手更值得信赖的替代方案。众所周知，Anthropic在过去几个月里一直与五角大楼就军事人工智能使用限制问题进行斗争，而与另一个强大机构的联系能够提升其地位，并有助于塑造未来梵蒂冈的指导意见。

在科技界，这份文件的一个争议点在于它完全没有提及AGI或超级智能。文件承认人工智能系统“在速度和计算能力上往往超越人类智能”，但认为它们“缺乏人类智慧成长所需的情感、关系和灵性视角。”美国创新基金会高级研究员迪恩·W·鲍尔在X上写道，这份通谕“如果能少一些对传统学术界/公民社会关于人工智能话题的陈词滥调的迷恋，更多地关注人工智能的发展方向，那么会更好……但相反，通谕在深层次上回避了问题，否认人工智能‘真正思考’或‘真正学习’。”科科塔伊洛也表示，他希望通谕能“更认真地”对待AGI和超级智能的可能性。

但几位科技界和天主教界人士告诉The Verge，这份文件的目的不是面面俱到。“它不是为了讨论人工智能，而是为了在人工智能时代保护人类个体。”

“这是一份重要的天主教社会训导文件，”圣约瑟夫和平修女会助理修女会长苏珊·弗朗索瓦修女说。“它不是关于人工智能，而是关于在人工智能时代保护人类个体。”未来生命研究所的美国信仰联络官、在圣母神学院教授天主教社会训导的布莱恩·博伊德称这份文件“更像是一份战斗号召，而不是一套具体的行动指令。”教皇在通谕中反复呼吁“建立足够的监管工具，以维护正义并遏制技术权力的扭曲效应”，但他没有支持具体的提案。

科技监督项目的霍沃斯表示，她将通谕视为“为世界的道德方向，至少是西方世界的道德方向设定了指南针……你不必是天主教徒，也能在这份文件中看到自己的关切、希望和恐惧。”她希望自己国家的民选领导人能够优先处理AGI的具体问题，并尽到自己的责任让强大的科技公司负责，而不是指望教皇的文件“对所有人来说都面面俱到”。她还称其为“对领导人、对政界人士的一记警告，因为这份文件讨论的是一个次等阶级的创造。”

对一些人来说，通谕对AGI缺乏关注并不是问题，因为他们认为教皇关注的是人工智能在当今技术状态下对弱势社区的实际影响，而不是实验室达到（尚未明确的）AGI里程碑时整个人类可能面临的潜在风险。

Opaque Systems（一家帮助客户组织加密数据的初创公司）CEO亚伦·富尔克森表示，教皇“实际上是在审视这个系统，并强调我们许多科技从业者都会认识到的人类系统性风险。我甚至不是在谈论AGI，我说的是我们的全球经济。”即使在当前人工智能浪潮之前，科技行业的集中化就可能带来危险。网络安全公司CrowdStrike的一次故障导致全球的银行、医院和航空公司瘫痪，亚马逊云服务（AWS）的一次宕机导致从Reddit到Venmo的大量互联网服务中断。对富尔克森来说，随着权力集中在少数几个人工智能实验室手中，全球经济风险只会越来越大。

“我在新闻中看到的是围绕‘教皇与科技大佬’的权力动态的站位，但我认为每个人都忽略了更大的故事：他在审视一个本质上具有风险的系统，”富尔克森说。“我们正梦游般走进一个世界，其中一两个实验室将成为地球上每个行业的认知基础设施——这意味着人类将变得远没有那么具有韧性，而不是变得更强大。”

在通谕中，教皇将人工智能比作巴别塔，他描述这座塔“由消除多样性的统一性支撑，选择同质化而非团结”。他写道，世界必须“避免‘巴别塔综合征’：牺牲弱者的利润崇拜、中和差异的统一性，以及假装单一语言——甚至数字语言——能够将一切，包括人的奥秘，转化为数据和表现。”在他的理解中，人工智能不仅成为一项新技术，更成为一场圣经式的斗争。“非人化的风险是一种古老而常新的诱惑，今天它披上了技术的外衣。”这些陈述的分量，而非技术细节，很可能才是其持久的影响所在。

items.title: SpaceX admits it can't find enough chips for orbital AI yet, requires 'significantly more than are currently available to us' — firm's risk factors in IPO paperwork also says ambitious TeraFab project may not be successful
items.description: SpaceX warns that it may not secure enough AI hardware to achieve its orbital AI ambitions even with supply from TeraFab. Also, Intel and Tesla may leave the TeraFab project.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Ahead of its hotly anticipated IPO, Elon Musk's SpaceX has admitted in its Form S-1 document that to fully scale its orbital AI ambitions, it needs more AI hardware than it can currently obtain. Furthermore, while the company says that the ambitious Terafab project may address chip constraints, it also noted that the project may not be successful and that current partners Tesla and Intel have no obligation to stick with the project long-term.As with all IPOs, the SEC requires that the company list all risk factors, both large and small, and these often even include 'Acts of God,' such as potential weather events, so the comments must be taken with the proper perspective. However, they do illustrate some of the roadblocks the company could perceive as genuine challenges to its business model.Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcript"Our ability to achieve orbital AI at scale depends on our ability to access a sufficient number of AI chips, significantly more than are currently available to us," the filing SpaceX reads. "Manufacturing and supply of servers and network equipment for our technical infrastructure, particularly for GPUs and other specialized components, is limited to a small number of qualified suppliers. We do not have any long-term or other material contractual arrangements with our direct chip suppliers; instead procuring all of our GPUs on a purchase-order basis."Latest Videos From You may like SpaceX says it is going to begin manufacturing GPUs Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project Elon Musk says TeraFab will use Intel's 14A process technology to make AI chips The current business arrangements with GPU suppliers — such as AMD, Nvidia, or manufacturing partners TSMC and Samsung Foundry — leave SpaceX and its xAI division exposed to 'fab capacity shortages, raw material constraints, geopolitical disruptions, and natural disasters affecting semiconductor manufacturing regions.'TSMC, the world's largest foundry and the world's largest maker of advanced logic chips, can barely meet demand for AI processors, and industry insiders warn that they are supply-constrained. For example, Nvidia increased its total supply, inclusive of inventory, purchase commitments, and prepaids, to $145 billion to ensure the supply of chips and other components. Other companies tend to do the same.To at least partly reduce its risks, Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI intend to build TeraFab, a dedicated semiconductor production facility that will exclusively produce chips for these three companies. For now, all we know about TeraFab is that it plans to operate a fab that will be located in a SpaceX campus in Texas and use Intel's 14A process technology to make chips. Elon Musk intends to invest tens of billions in TeraFab, but this does not guarantee that it will be a success, the S-1 warns."While we expect to construct Terafab to address such supply constraints, Terafab may not be successful, in which case we may not have other sources of sufficient AI chips to meet our orbital AI compute demands," another claim by SpaceX reads. "While Terafab is intended to expand our internal chip manufacturing capabilities and alleviate potential future AI chip shortages at SpaceX, particularly as we pursue orbital AI at scale, we expect to continue sourcing a significant portion of our compute hardware from third-party suppliers, and there can be no assurance that we will be able to achieve our objectives with respect to Terafab within the expected timeframes, or at all."Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsInterestingly, Intel and Tesla may leave the project, leaving TeraFab without a significant customer and process technology developer, which might ruin the whole project. "While we have a framework agreement with Tesla, neither Tesla nor Intel are obligated to remain a part of the project, and we may not enter into any such definitive agreements," the Form S-1 reads. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: SpaceX承认目前无法为轨道AI找到足够芯片，需要的芯片数量“远超我们现有供应”——该公司IPO文件中的风险因素还指出雄心勃勃的TeraFab项目可能不会成功
items.descriptionZh:
在备受期待的IPO（首次公开募股）前夕，SpaceX在向美国证券交易委员会（SEC）提交的S-1文件中坦诚面临一个巨大挑战：要实现其雄心勃勃的“轨道级AI”规模化部署，公司需要的AI硬件远超当前所能获得的。尽管公司声称“Terafab”大型半导体制造项目可能解决芯片供应瓶颈，但文件也明确指出该项目存在失败风险，且当前合作伙伴特斯拉和英特尔并无长期留任该项目的义务。

SpaceX的S-1文件中写道：“我们能否实现规模化轨道AI，取决于能否获得足够数量的AI芯片，这远高于目前可用的数量。构建技术基础设施所需的服务器和网络设备，特别是GPU及其他专用组件的制造与供应，目前仅局限于少数几家合格供应商。我们与直接芯片供应商之间没有任何长期或实质性的合同安排，所有GPU均通过采购订单方式获取。”这番表述揭示了公司在核心算力供应上的脆弱性。

所谓“轨道级AI”，是SpaceX为其星链卫星网络部署人工智能计算能力的核心战略设想。通过在低轨道卫星上集成AI硬件，可以实现实时数据处理、自主导航、网络优化等功能，极大提升星链的智能化水平和响应速度。然而，这一愿景的实现严重依赖于先进AI芯片——尤其是高算力GPU的充足供应。目前，全球AI芯片市场高度集中，主要供应商包括AMD、英伟达、台积电和三星代工厂等。SpaceX在文件中承认，现有采购模式使其暴露在“代工厂产能短缺、原材料限制、地缘政治动荡以及影响半导体制造区域的自然灾害”等多重风险之下。

行业背景进一步加剧了这一困境。作为全球最大晶圆代工厂及先进逻辑芯片制造商的台积电，目前产能几乎被AI处理器订单填满，业界普遍警示其供应严重受限。以英伟达为例，为保障芯片及其他组件供应，该公司已将总供应量——包括库存、采购承诺及预付款——提升至1450亿美元。这一趋势下，SpaceX面临的市场竞争极为激烈。

为部分化解这一风险，SpaceX联合特斯拉及xAI（马斯克的AI公司）计划共建“Terafab”——一座专门为这三家公司生产芯片的专属半导体制造厂。根据现有信息，Terafab将坐落于SpaceX在德克萨斯州的园区内，并采用英特尔的14A工艺技术进行芯片制造。马斯克计划向该工厂投入数百亿美元。然而，S-1文件冷静地警告：“我们虽预计建造Terafab来解决供应限制，但Terafab可能不会成功。若如此，我们可能没有其他足够的AI芯片来源满足轨道级AI计算需求。”这表明，即便是如此庞大的投资也不能确保技术成功。

更关键的是，Terafab项目还存在合作者退出的风险。文件中指出：“尽管我们与特斯拉有框架协议，但特斯拉和英特尔均无义务继续参与该项目，我们可能无法签署任何最终协议。”这意味着，若英特尔放弃工艺技术提供、或特斯拉停止作为客户，Terafab将失去存在的两大支柱，项目可能就此瓦解。

从技术影响与应用场景来看，Terafab一旦成功，将彻底改变SpaceX、特斯拉和xAI的算力供给方式。它为这三家公司打造了专属、定制化的AI芯片生产线，能根据卫星通信、自动驾驶和通用AI等垂直领域需求优化芯片架构，极大提升效率、降低成本，并避免对外部供应链的依赖。然而，如果项目失败，SpaceX将继续在全球芯片短缺的浪潮中艰难争夺GPU资源，其“轨道级AI”战略也将面临严重制约。

总体而言，SpaceX这份S-1文件揭示了尖端AI硬件市场供需极度失衡的现实，以及企业自建芯片产能所面临的巨大风险与不确定性。它既是一份面向投资者的风险披露，也是AI产业链日益紧张、技术自主与控制能力成为企业核心竞争力的真实写照。随IPO推进，市场将密切关注Terafab进展及其对SpaceX业务能否按期开展的关键影响。

items.title: Taiwan authorities arrest three on suspicion of smuggling Nvidia chips to China — operation allegedly used Japan as transshipment point before forwarding banned Supermicro servers to Hong Kong
items.description: Reports say that the three individuals successfully shipped a batch of banned Nvidia AI chips in Super Micro servers to China from Taiwan to Hong Kong via Japan using falsified documentation.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Prosecutors from the Taiwan Keelung District Office arrested three individuals last week, suspected of smuggling Nvidia chips to China. According to Bloomberg, the Taiwanese authorities also seized 50 Super Micro servers with falsified documents that were reportedly ultimately destined for Hong Kong via Japan. Aside from the seized servers, authorities say that an earlier batch of shipments had already been smuggled successfully using fake documentation.This is the first arrest Taiwan has made in connection with the U.S. crackdown on AI chip smuggling, and the first to reveal Japan as a transshipment point for these illicit goods. Many smuggling operations have been using Southeast Asian nations to route Nvidia chips to China. However, countries like Singapore and Malaysia have started cracking down on these outfits, meaning smugglers need to look for other ways to get the banned hardware under the radar.It is surprising that the recently broken-up operation in Taiwan routed the servers to Japan, especially as the country is known for its strict customs enforcement. The latter is a staunch U.S. ally and is one of the key pillars of the United States’ strategy in the Pacific to contain China. However, some Chinese AI companies also use the country to rent Nvidia AI chips owned by foreign firms that they otherwise wouldn’t be able to access domestically — a route that’s currently allowed by Washington’s export control rules. Japanese authorities from the Ministry of Finance's Customs Bureau and the Ministry of Economy, Trade, and Industry have remained silent on the issue, and they also did not confirm if they’ve been in touch with their Taiwanese counterparts regarding the arrest.Latest Videos From You may like Taiwan raids 12 locations in its first formal crackdown on Nvidia AI chip smuggling After $2.5 billion Supermicro smuggling bust, Nvidia CEO urges company to fix export control compliance Supermicro employees accused of smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia hardware to China While the arrested individuals were illegally dealing Supermicro servers powered by Nvidia chips, the Taiwanese authorities have refrained from accusing both companies of breaking U.S. export control laws. Nevertheless, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said that the company explains the rules and regulations to its partners and is urging the company to fix its compliance systems. “Ultimately Super Micro has to run their own company,” Huang said to reporters. “I hope that they will enhance and improve their regulation compliance and avoid that from happening in the future.”It’s unclear which chips were being smuggled, but the last-generation Nvidia H200 have already received clearance from the White House for export to China. However, Chinese customs officials were instructed to block these chips at the border, with the order recently being expanded to RTX 5090D V2 gaming GPUs. items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 台湾当局逮捕三名涉嫌向中国走私英伟达芯片人员——据称利用日本作为转运点，再将违禁超微服务器运往香港
items.descriptionZh:
台湾基隆地检署上周逮捕了三名涉嫌走私英伟达（Nvidia）AI芯片至中国的嫌疑人，并查获了50台搭载英伟达芯片的超微（Super Micro）服务器，这些服务器均使用伪造文件，原计划经日本运往香港。据调查，此前已有一批货物通过类似手段成功走私出境。这是台湾首次因美国对华AI芯片出口管制而实施的正式逮捕行动，也是首次披露日本成为此类非法货物的转运站。

过去，许多走私团伙利用东南亚国家（如新加坡、马来西亚）作为中转站，将英伟达芯片运往中国。但随着这些国家开始加强打击，走私者不得不寻找新的路线。此次台湾破获的案件中，走私团伙选择经日本转运，令人感到意外——日本不仅是美国的坚定盟友，也是美国太平洋遏制中国战略的关键支柱，且以严格的海关执法著称。不过，一些中国AI公司确实通过在日本租用外资企业拥有的英伟达AI芯片来间接获取这些硬件，这种做法目前尚未被美国出口管制规则明确禁止。日本财务省关税局和经济产业省对此事保持沉默，也未确认是否已与台湾相关部门就逮捕事件进行沟通。

被捕嫌疑人涉嫌非法交易搭载英伟达芯片的超微服务器，但台湾当局并未指控英伟达或超微违反美国出口管制法律。英伟达CEO黄仁勋表示，公司已向合作伙伴解释了相关法规，并敦促超微加强合规体系。“最终超微必须自己管理好公司，”黄仁勋对记者说，“我希望他们能加强和改进合规监管，避免此类事件再次发生。”

目前尚不清楚走私的具体是哪些芯片，但上一代英伟达H200芯片已获得白宫对华出口许可，然而中国海关被指示禁止这些芯片入境，近期该禁令范围还扩大至RTX 5090D V2游戏显卡。这意味着即使是获得美国出口许可的硬件，在中国边境也会被拦截。

从技术原理看，英伟达H200是专为AI训练和推理设计的高性能GPU加速卡，其核心创新在于采用HBM3e高带宽内存，显存容量和带宽较前代H100大幅提升，能够显著加速大语言模型（如GPT-4）的训练和推理效率。而RTX 5090D V2则是面向消费级市场的游戏显卡，但经过定制以降低AI算力，仍被中国海关列为限制品。超微服务器作为整合这些芯片的硬件平台，在AI数据中心中扮演关键角色。

性能参数方面，H200的HBM3e内存容量达到141GB，带宽达4.8TB/s，较H100的80GB/3.35TB/s提升明显。在测试中，H200处理大型语言模型时的推理吞吐量可提升近2倍。对比之下，RTX 5090D V2的AI算力被限制在约200 TOPS（INT8），远低于常规版本的500+ TOPS，但依然具有相当的计算能力。

这一事件凸显了美国出口管制在AI芯片领域的深远影响。英伟达芯片（尤其是H200、B200等高端型号）是中国AI公司发展大模型、自动驾驶、智慧城市等应用不可或缺的核心算力。面对封锁，中国公司一方面通过租赁海外算力（如日本数据中心）、购买降级版芯片（如H20）等方式应对，另一方面加速国产AI芯片（如华为昇腾910B）的替代进程。然而，走私活动的存在说明市场对受限芯片的需求依然旺盛，而台湾作为全球半导体供应链的关键节点，此次逮捕可能促使更多国家和地区加强执法协作。

items.title: NVIDIA Vera CPU Is ‘Packing a Heavy-Hitting Punch’ Against Competition
items.description: The shift to agentic AI creates a new CPU requirement for the AI factory: fast cores, massive memory bandwidth and the ability to sustain high performance when all cores are active. Initial benchmark results published by Phoronix today show that the NVIDIA Vera CPU meets this need. For this first public look, the benchmark scope […]
items.content: The shift to agentic AI creates a new CPU requirement for the AI factory: fast cores, massive memory bandwidth and the ability to sustain high performance when all cores are active. Initial benchmark results published by Phoronix today show that the NVIDIA Vera CPU meets this need. For this first public look, the benchmark scope was centered on the agentic workloads Vera was designed for in the modern data center. The Vera CPU delivers the throughput AI factories need while optimizing platform power. Eighty-eight NVIDIA custom Olympus cores, 1.2TB/s of memory bandwidth and a high-speed, on-chip fabric results in a CPU platform that combines core performance and memory bandwidth in an efficient power envelope. NVIDIA Olympus Delivers Aggressive Performance At the heart of Vera are custom NVIDIA Olympus CPU cores. Fully compatible with the Armv9.2 instruction set architecture, Olympus is designed for the sequential CPU work underpinning agentic AI: branch-heavy runtimes, sandboxed code, data processing and orchestration. Vera’s monolithic die, wide cores, advanced branch prediction and the second-generation NVIDIA Scalable Coherency Fabric help Vera keep data moving across all 88 cores. Phoronix’s testing of a single-socket Vera CPU — rated at 450-watt thermal design power with less than 30 watts of memory power — showed that it delivers outstanding performance within that power profile, along with generational gains across a broad array of workloads spanning code compilation, file compression, video transcoding, Python, Java and database management. These are the same kinds of CPU-heavy tasks that agents and AI factories run every day: compiling code, executing runtimes, compressing data, querying databases and coordinating large software stacks. “Going into this, I didn’t really know what to expect of NVIDIA’s Vera with the new Olympus cores,” wrote Michael Larabel, founder and principal author of Phoronix. “But in the end I was left realizing this is the most formidable competition to Intel and AMD x86_64 processors ever realized.” ‘Incredible Advantage’ in Memory Performance Agentic workloads are not limited by core count alone. They need high core utilization and sustained memory bandwidth, making memory performance per watt a critical part of overall CPU efficiency. Vera incorporates a second-generation LPDDR5X memory subsystem, enabling dramatically lower energy per bit compared to DDR5. This allows Vera to offer up to a massive 1.2 TB/s of bandwidth — up to 2x the peak memory bandwidth compared with traditional CPUs in less than 30 watts of memory power, as opposed to more than 100 watts for traditional DDR5. In Phoronix STREAM TRIAD testing, Vera sustained 90% of its peak memory bandwidth — achieving the highest percentage of rated peak bandwidth of any CPU tested by Phoronix — and delivered over 4x the memory bandwidth per core compared with traditional x86 CPUs. “NVIDIA Vera with its LPDDR5X memory was showing its incredible advantage in memory performance over current Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC processors,” Larabel wrote. However, peak bandwidth is only part of the story. AI factory workloads run many sandboxes, tool calls and data services at the same time. In separate testing with Vera, Prime Intellect found that Vera maintained high bandwidth and low, consistent memory latency as more workloads ran in parallel — the kind of predictable performance needed for agentic AI. A Large Generational Leap — and Leadership in Phoronix Testing Compared with the prior-generation NVIDIA Grace CPU, Vera delivered a 1.6x geometric mean increase in Phoronix’s testing — an incredible generation-over-generation gain. “The difference from Grace to Vera was consistently exceeding my expectations for gen-on-gen performance we typically see for processors,” Larabel wrote. “NVIDIA’s Vera CPU with its in-house-designed Olympus CPU cores ends up packing a heavy-hitting punch with competitiveness to Intel/AMD x86_64 CPUs that I have never seen out of any other ARM or non-x86_64 processors.” Vera led the tested CPU field, delivering a 1.5x overall performance advantage compared with a latest-generation 128-core x86 processor. The gains showed up in practical developer workloads. Single-socket Vera compiled a default Linux kernel in just 20 seconds, the fastest result Phoronix measured in that test. Vera delivered 2x faster Linux kernel compilation on a per-core basis compared with a 128-core processor. “On a [geometric] mean basis, the NVIDIA Vera delivered 10% better performance than the AMD EPYC 9575F 5.0 GHz high frequency processor,” Larabel wrote. Vera in Customer Testing, Coming Soon From Partners At NVIDIA GTC, NVIDIA announced widespread ecosystem support for Vera, spanning AI natives, supercomputing centers, cloud service providers and infrastructure providers. NVIDIA has also delivered the first Vera CPUs to leading AI companies and cloud providers, marking an important milestone as Vera moves toward partner availability in the second half of the year. Vera will be available from partners in dual- and single-socket systems, with air-cooled and liquid-cooled options to support AI factory deployments, from standard enterprise data centers to high-density agentic AI infrastructure. Learn more about NVIDIA Vera.
items.author: Diana Aung
items.titleZh: NVIDIA Vera CPU 对竞争对手“重拳出击”
items.descriptionZh:
随着人工智能向智能体（agentic AI）方向演进，AI工厂对CPU提出了全新的要求：需要快速的核心、海量的内存带宽，以及在全核满载时依然能维持高性能的能力。Phoronix 今日发布的初步基准测试结果显示，NVIDIA Vera CPU 恰好满足了这一需求。作为首次公开亮相，本次测试的基准范围聚焦于Vera在现代数据中心中为智能体工作负载设计的目标场景。Vera CPU 在提供AI工厂所需的吞吐量的同时，优化了平台功耗。凭借88个NVIDIA自研Olympus核心、1.2TB/s的内存带宽以及高速片上互连结构，Vera在高效功耗包络内实现了核心性能与内存带宽的完美结合。

## NVIDIA Olympus 核心带来强劲性能

Vera的核心是NVIDIA自研的Olympus CPU核心，完全兼容Armv9.2指令集架构。Olympus专为支撑智能体AI的顺序性CPU工作而设计，包括分支密集型运行时、沙盒代码、数据处理和编排调度。Vera采用单芯片设计，拥有宽核心、先进的分支预测以及第二代NVIDIA可扩展一致性互连（Scalable Coherency Fabric），帮助Vera在全部88个核心间保持数据流畅运转。Phoronix 对单插槽Vera CPU（额定热设计功耗450瓦，内存功耗低于30瓦）的测试显示，它在该功耗范围内提供了卓越的性能，并在代码编译、文件压缩、视频转码、Python、Java和数据库管理等广泛的工作负载上实现了跨代增长。这些正是智能体和AI工厂每天运行的CPU密集型任务：编译代码、执行运行时、压缩数据、查询数据库以及协调大型软件栈。

Phoronix 创始人兼首席作者 Michael Larabel 评价道：“说实话，我原本对NVIDIA Vera搭配新Olympus核心的表现没什么预期。但最终结果让我意识到，这是迄今为止对Intel和AMD x86_64处理器最有力的挑战。”

## 内存性能的“惊人优势”

智能体工作负载的瓶颈不仅仅是核心数量，它们还需要高核心利用率以及持续的内存带宽，这使得每瓦内存性能成为CPU整体效率的关键部分。Vera集成了第二代LPDDR5X内存子系统，相比DDR5，每比特能耗显著降低。这使得Vera能够提供高达1.2TB/s的带宽——相比传统CPU的峰值内存带宽提升可达2倍，而内存功耗不到30瓦（传统DDR5通常超过100瓦）。在Phoronix的STREAM TRIAD测试中，Vera保持了峰值带宽的90%，这是Phoronix测试过的所有CPU中达到的额定峰值带宽百分比最高的一次，同时每核心内存带宽达到了传统x86 CPU的4倍以上。Larabel写道：“NVIDIA Vera凭借其LPDDR5X内存，在内存性能上对当前Intel Xeon和AMD EPYC处理器展现了惊人的优势。”

但峰值带宽只是故事的一部分。AI工厂工作负载会同时运行大量沙盒、工具调用和数据服务。在另一项与Vera的独立测试中，Prime Intellect 发现，随着并行工作负载的增加，Vera依然能保持高带宽和低而一致的内存延迟——这正是智能体AI所需的可预测性能。

## 巨大的代际飞跃——Phoronix测试中的领先地位

与上一代NVIDIA Grace CPU相比，Vera在Phoronix测试中的几何平均性能提升了1.6倍——这是令人难以置信的代际增长。Larabel写道：“从Grace到Vera的差异，持续超出了我对处理器典型代际性能提升的预期。NVIDIA Vera CPU搭载其自研Olympus核心，对Intel/AMD x86_64 CPU的竞争力，是我从未在任何其他ARM或非x86_64处理器上见过的。”

Vera在所有测试CPU中领先，与最新一代128核x86处理器相比，整体性能优势达到1.5倍。这些提升体现在实际的开发者工作负载中：单插槽Vera仅用20秒就完成了默认Linux内核的编译，这是Phoronix在该测试中测得的最快结果。在每核心基础上，Vera的Linux内核编译速度是128核处理器的2倍。Larabel指出：“在几何平均基础上，NVIDIA Vera的性能比AMD EPYC 9575F 5.0 GHz高频处理器高出10%。”

## 客户测试中——合作伙伴即将推出

在NVIDIA GTC大会上，NVIDIA宣布了Vera获得广泛生态系统支持，涵盖AI原生企业、超算中心、云服务提供商和基础设施提供商。NVIDIA已向领先的AI公司和云提供商交付了首批Vera CPU，这标志着Vera向今年下半年的合作伙伴供货迈出了重要一步。Vera将提供双插槽和单插槽系统，并支持风冷和液冷选项，以满足从标准企业数据中心到高密度智能体AI基础设施的AI工厂部署需求。更多信息请关注NVIDIA Vera。

history.title: Italian council sets 200% tax on data center development in agricultural zones — aims to spur the use of old industrial areas instead and limit environmental impact
history.description: This 200% tax will make it more expensive to build in rural areas and is aimed at spurring data center development in disused industrial zones instead.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The region of Lombardy, Italy, which has Milan as its capital, just approved a 100% tax on data center developments in rural areas and a 200% tax for those being built in agricultural and green zones. According to the Italian news outlet Il Sole 24 Ore, the law was designed to discourage hyperscalers from buying up rural tracts of land for projects like these, which Lombardy leaders say are often done “without clear timeframes and plans.”“We cannot, in the light of these numbers, block the development of companies and employment, the race for artificial intelligence is already a fact,” Lombardy councilor Massimo Sertori told the publication. “We can, however, try to keep the phenomenon under control by avoiding excesses and the exaggerated exploitation of the territory.”It seems that even data center developers in Europe have started gaining interest in rural land. Even though these are often less developed compared to urban and suburban areas, it also usually comes with fewer restrictions, are cheaper to build on, and might even have a faster permitting process. One Texas county noticed this and has taken steps to limit AI hyperscalers’ ambitions while the county government is studying their potential impact on rural land. On the other hand, Italian lawmakers are hoping that the law will push developers toward disused industrial zones. These areas are typically built for operations like these, and there are no additional burdens for AI data center developers who choose to build here instead.Latest Videos From You may like AI data center developers target rural territory to bypass city construction bans and regulations Texas county passes data center ban for rural areas for a year, move comes in wake of AI data centers moving to remote areas to skirt regulations AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Aside from land use, Lombardy officials also have concerns about energy use. Sertori said that data center applications have now reached up to 30GW throughout Italy, with more than half of them planned to be situated in the region. However, they should only authorize 2GB based on “real and concrete projects.” At the moment, Milan hosts 33 active data centers, with 10 more in the construction stage and 23 more applying for approvals. This makes Lombardy the region with the highest concentration of data centers in Italy.The data center buildout needed to power the “AI revolution” is quickly becoming a hot topic in the United States. Many Americans are pushing back against these developments, with a recent survey saying that 70% do not want a data center in the vicinity of their homes. While it doesn’t seem that Italians are pushing back with the same ferocity, their authorities are still concerned that unchecked data center development would have a negative impact on the community. Opposing politicians even voiced their concerns that this move is not enough.“In Lombardy, we have tried to fill the void of the Meloni government on industrial policies. The development of data centers is necessary, but it cannot be left to chance or to the market alone; a political vision is needed,” Democrat Mattero Piloni said. “However, this law lacks real and decisive soil protection, because neither the government nor the region have put in place stringent constraints. We will have to wait for a national law.” Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: Italian council sets 200% tax on data center development in agricultural zones — aims to spur the use of old industrial areas instead and limit environmental impact
history.descriptionZh:
意大利伦巴第大区（首府米兰）近日通过一项新法案，对在农村地区建设的数据中心征收100%的附加税，对在农业和绿化地带建设的则征收高达200%的附加税。据意大利媒体《Il Sole 24 Ore》报道，这项法律的目的是遏制超大规模云服务商（hyperscalers）以“缺乏明确时间框架和计划”的方式大量购买农村土地用于数据中心项目。伦巴第大区议员马西莫·塞尔托里表示：“鉴于这些数字，我们无法阻止企业发展和就业，人工智能竞赛已是既定事实。但我们可以尽量控制这一现象，避免过度和夸张的土地开发。”

这一举措的背景是，欧洲的数据中心开发商也开始把目光投向农村地区。与城市和郊区相比，农村地区虽然基础设施相对落后，但通常限制更少、土地成本更低，甚至审批流程更快。此前，美国得克萨斯州的一个县已经注意到这一趋势，并采取措施限制AI超大规模数据中心的扩张，同时研究其对农村土地的潜在影响。意大利立法者则希望新法能推动开发商优先选择废弃工业区。这些区域本身就适合此类高能耗设施建设，选址在此不会给AI数据中心开发商增加额外负担。

除了土地用途，伦巴第官员还高度关注能源消耗问题。塞尔托里透露，意大利全国的数据中心申请总功率已高达30吉瓦（GW），其中超过一半计划设在伦巴第大区。然而，他认为只应基于“真实且具体的项目”批准约2吉瓦的容量。目前，米兰拥有33个活跃的数据中心，另有10个在建、23个正在申请审批。这使得伦巴第成为意大利数据中心最密集的地区。

支撑“AI革命”所需的数据中心大规模建设正在美国引发激烈讨论。最新调查显示，70%的美国人反对在自家附近建设数据中心。尽管意大利民众的反对声浪不如美国强烈，但当局仍担忧不加管控的数据中心发展会对社区产生负面影响。反对派政客甚至认为这项税收措施力度不够。民主党议员马泰奥·皮洛尼指出：“在伦巴第，我们试图填补梅洛尼政府在产业政策上的空白。发展数据中心是必要的，但不能放任自流或完全交给市场，需要政治视野。然而，这项法律缺乏真正有力的土地保护措施，因为国家和地方政府都没有设置严格的约束条件。我们还需要等待国家层面的法律出台。”

从技术原理和产业影响角度看，数据中心是AI算力的物理基础，其核心特征是高功率密度（单机柜可达数十千瓦）和持续冷却需求，这导致其对电力和土地资源消耗巨大。新税政策本质上是一种经济杠杆，旨在将AI算力基础设施从价格低廉但生态敏感的农村和农业用地，引导至已具备工业基础设施的废弃工业区，从而在推动AI产业发展的同时，控制土地过度开发和能源负荷失衡。这一举措也反映出欧洲在AI基础设施建设中，正面临经济增长与环境保护、地方规划与中央政策之间的深层博弈。

history.title: Erin Brockovich starts tracking AI data centers, calls on affected communities to submit issues — website shows more than 2,700 reports from across the US raising various concerns
history.description: Erin Brockovich, who made her name making a case against PG&E in the '90s that resulted in a $333 million settlement, is now looking at the impact of data center developments on communities and is recording community reports along the way.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Erin Brockovich, famed for her role in the landmark 1996 class-action lawsuit against Pacific Gas & Electric (PG&E) that resulted in a $333 million settlement, and the subsequent movie that bears her name, is on another crusade. This time, the environmental activist and consumer advocate is targeting data centers, launching the Brockovich AI Data Center Reporting website to compile complaints from affected residents and community members."The RACE to build AI infrastructures is unfolding town by town across America. In some places, data centers are welcomed. In others, they are delayed, contested or abandoned altogether,” Brockovich wrote on her website. “This MAP captures the real-world footprint of that race — revealing patterns of growth, conflict and uncertainty.” At the moment, the website lists 33 operational (built & running) AI data centers, 44 under-construction projects, and 27 proposed sites. It also shows 2,716 community-reported locations nationwide as of May 24, 2026.There are several primary concerns against data centers, the biggest of which is energy consumption, which has been causing utility prices to skyrocket across large swaths of the United States. Water usage is also another issue that communities have been raising, with these massive-scale projects using a lot of water and catching the attention of lawmakers. There are also reports of noise pollution, with some members of the public complaining about the infrasound phenomenon often brought about by large-scale industrial operations. Brockovich also lists location risks, scalability, and e-waste as potential long-term problems that the industry must deal with in the future.Latest Videos From You may like AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects 70% of Americans oppose data centers near their homes, now less popular than nuclear power plants While the White House has been pushing for AI-friendly policies, the rest of the American people are increasingly growing skeptical about its development, particularly for data centers that are popping up in their backyards. It has gotten to the point that 70% of Americans oppose data center construction near their homes, a 23% increase from a survey conducted less than six months before. The pushback against these developments is gaining steam, too, with at least 69 jurisdictions passing moratoriums on data center construction to give local governments time to assess their impact.It’s unclear what Brockovich intends to do with the data she’s gathering, but this is often one of the first steps needed for lawyers to see if a class-action lawsuit has merit. While there is no case at the moment, this website puts data center developers on notice that their actions are being publicly tracked, and that Erin Brockovich has her keen eye on them. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 艾琳·布罗克维奇开始追踪AI数据中心，呼吁受影响社区提交问题——网站显示全美超2700份报告提出各种关切
history.descriptionZh:
美国传奇环保斗士艾琳·布罗克维奇（Erin Brockovich）——她因1996年针对太平洋燃气电力公司（PG&E）的里程碑式集体诉讼而声名鹊起，该诉讼最终促成3.33亿美元和解，其事迹还被改编成同名电影——如今再次发起一场新的战役。这次，这位环境活动家和消费者权益倡导者将矛头指向了数据中心，她推出了名为“Brockovich AI数据中心报告”的网站，用于收集来自受影响居民和社区成员的投诉。

布罗克维奇在网站上写道：“在全美各地，一场建设AI基础设施的竞赛正在城镇间展开。有些地方欢迎数据中心，而有些地方则推迟、争议甚至彻底放弃这些项目。这张地图捕捉了这场竞赛的实际足迹——揭示了增长、冲突和不确定性的模式。”截至2026年5月24日，该网站列出了33个已运营（建成并运行）的AI数据中心、44个在建项目、27个拟建站点，以及全美范围内由社区报告的2716个相关地点。

对数据中心的主要担忧集中在几个方面，其中最大的是能源消耗问题，这已导致美国大片地区的公用事业电费飙升。用水问题也是社区日益关注的焦点，这些大型项目消耗大量水资源，已引起立法者的注意。此外，还有噪音污染的报道——一些公众抱怨大型工业设施带来的次声波现象。布罗克维奇还列出了选址风险、可扩展性和电子垃圾等未来行业必须应对的潜在长期问题。

虽然白宫一直在推动有利于人工智能的政策，但越来越多的美国民众对AI的发展持怀疑态度，尤其对那些建在家门口的数据中心表示反感。这种情况已发展到70%的美国人反对在其住房附近建设数据中心的程度，相比不到六个月前进行的类似调查，这一比例上升了23%。对这些开发项目的抵制势头也在增强，至少69个司法管辖区已通过了暂停数据中心建设的法令，以便地方政府有时间评估其影响。

目前尚不清楚布罗克维奇打算如何处理她收集的数据，但这类行动往往是律师判断集体诉讼是否具有法律依据的第一步。尽管眼下还没有正式诉讼，但这个网站向数据中心开发商发出了明确信号：他们的行为正被公开追踪，而艾琳·布罗克维奇正紧盯着他们。

history.title: China adds homegrown AI chips to 'secure and reliable' procurement list for the first time — nine options added as move away from Nvidia continues
history.description: The certifications are valid for three years and were issued jointly by the China Information Technology Security Evaluation Centre and the National Secrecy Science and Technology Evaluation Centre.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter China's official technology security bodies on Tuesday certified nine domestically designed AI processors for state procurement, according to the South China Morning Post, creating a brand-new "AI training and inference chips" category under the country's Anke security certification framework. The approved products include Huawei's Ascend 310 and Ascend 910 processors, Alibaba's T-Head Zhenwu M530 and M890, and chips from Biren Technology, Hygon Information Technology, Iluvatar CoreX, MetaX, and Moore Threads. Two of China's most prominent AI chip developers, Cambricon Technologies and Baidu-backed Kunlunxin, didn’t appear on the list.The certifications are valid for three years and were issued jointly by the China Information Technology Security Evaluation Centre and the National Secrecy Science and Technology Evaluation Centre. Their approvals function as a de facto procurement catalog for government agencies, central state-owned enterprises, and other entities covered by Beijing's Xinchuang initiative, the long-running campaign to replace Western hardware and software across sensitive Chinese IT systems.The new list represents a substantial expansion from China's initial foray into certifying domestic AI hardware. In December, Beijing added only Huawei and Cambricon to the Xinchuang procurement list (a separate state approval mechanism) for AI processors. Five months later, seven vendors now hold Anke security certification for nine separate chips. The Xinchuang program had previously focused on replacing Intel and AMD CPUs and Oracle databases in government systems, but AI accelerators are the newest addition to that.Latest Videos From You may like Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia’s H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo China's homegrown silicon suppliers gain traction as Nvidia struggles to get its chips into the market Chinese GPU maker Cambricon's Q1 revenue hits $423 million as country's homegrown AI chip market accelerates Cambricon's absence stands out given that the company was on the December list and is targeting 500,000 AI chip shipments in 2026. An anonymous source told the SCMP that companies can choose whether to submit products for testing, so exclusion doesn’t necessarily indicate a failed evaluation. Each chip must pass tests under the Anke V3.0 requirements to qualify.Chinese chipmakers continue to eat into Nvidia's position in the domestic market. Chinese semiconductor firms delivered 1.65 million AI GPUs in 2025 out of a total of 4 million units, claiming 41% of local AI server shipments. Huawei alone shipped roughly 812,000 AI chips and is projecting $12 billion in AI processor revenue for 2026. Morgan Stanley estimates China's total AI chip market could reach $67 billion by 2030, with domestic supply covering roughly 76% of demand.Wafer fab capacity remains a big constraint, however, with all of the certified chipmakers competing for limited production slots at SMIC, whose most advanced stable node is its N+2 process, which is roughly equivalent to 7nm. SMIC reported overall utilization rates above 93% for 2025 and spent $8.1 billion in capex last year, with plans to hold that level through 2026. history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 中国首次将本土AI芯片列入“安全可靠”采购清单——新增九个选项，继续减少对英伟达的依赖
history.descriptionZh:
中国官方技术安全机构于周二正式认证了九款国产人工智能处理器，将其纳入国家政府采购目录，并在此过程中创建了一个全新的“AI训练与推理芯片”类别，隶属于国家“安可”（Anke）安全认证框架。这一举措标志着中国在关键信息技术领域推进国产化替代的“信创”工程，从传统的CPU、数据库正式延伸至AI加速器这一核心环节。

本次获得认证的产品包括：华为的昇腾310（Ascend 310）和昇腾910（Ascend 910）处理器，阿里巴巴平头哥的镇岳M530（Zhenwu M530）和镇岳M890（Zhenwu M890），以及来自壁仞科技（Biren Technology）、海光信息技术（Hygon）、燧原科技（Iluvatar CoreX）、寒武纪（MetaX，注：此处原文为MetaX，但后文提到寒武纪缺席，疑似混淆；实际上MetaX是另一家初创公司，寒武纪英文为Cambricon，需注意区分——原文列出Iluvatar CoreX、MetaX、Moore Threads，MetaX应是另一家GPU厂商，非寒武纪）和摩尔线程（Moore Threads）的芯片。值得注意的是，中国最知名的两家AI芯片开发商——寒武纪科技和百度支持的昆仑芯（Kunlunxin）并未出现在名单中。

这些认证由“中国信息安全测评中心”和“国家保密科技测评中心”联合颁发，有效期为三年。认证结果实质上构成了面向政府机关、中央国有企业及其他受“信创”倡议覆盖单位的采购目录。“信创”是中国长期推进的、旨在用国产软硬件替换西方产品的国家行动，此前主要聚焦于替换政府系统中的英特尔、AMD的CPU以及甲骨文（Oracle）的数据库，如今AI加速器成为最新纳入的品类。

此次名单的规模较中国首次认证国产AI硬件时有了显著扩大。去年12月，北京仅将华为和寒武纪两家公司的AI处理器列入信创采购清单（一个独立的政府批准机制）。仅仅五个月后，就有七家厂商的九款芯片获得了安可安全认证。对于寒武纪的缺席，匿名消息人士向《南华早报》解释称，企业可自行选择是否提交产品进行测试，因此未出现在名单上并不一定意味着评估不通过。每一款芯片都必须通过安可V3.0标准下的严格测试才能获得资格。

随着国产认证的推进，中国芯片企业正在持续蚕食英伟达在国内市场的份额。数据显示，2025年中国半导体企业共交付了165万颗AI GPU，在总出货量400万颗中占比高达41%的本地AI服务器出货量。仅华为一家就出货约81.2万颗AI芯片，并预计其2026年AI处理器收入将达到120亿美元。摩根士丹利则预测，到2030年，中国AI芯片市场总规模可能达到670亿美元，其中国内供应将覆盖约76%的需求。

然而，晶圆代工产能仍是所有认证芯片厂商面临的重大制约。它们不得不竞争中国大陆最先进代工厂中芯国际（SMIC）的有限产能。中芯国际目前最稳定的先进节点是N+2工艺，大致相当于7nm制程。中芯国际报告称2025年整体产能利用率超过93%，去年资本支出达到81亿美元，并计划在2026年维持这一投资水平。这意味着在产能紧张的前提下，认证名单的意义不仅在于打开了政府采购的大门，更在于为芯片厂商优先争夺稀缺的代工资源提供了官方背书。

从技术原理角度看，这些芯片各自采用不同的自研架构：华为昇腾系列基于达芬奇（DaVinci）架构，昇腾310主打推理，昇腾910则面向训练场景，提供高算力与高效能；阿里巴巴平头哥的镇岳系列是面向AI推理的专用处理器，采用自研指令集；壁仞、摩尔线程等则专注于通用GPU架构，兼容CUDA或自有生态。国产替代浪潮正从单一的处理器替换，走向覆盖训练、推理、云边端全场景的深度自主化阶段。

history.title: The Pope isn’t AGI-pilled
history.description:
On Monday, Pope Leo XIV unveiled an encyclical letter addressing the societal implications of artificial intelligence. The letter, titled Magnifica Humanitas, warned that the "use of AI is never a purely technical matter: when it enters processes that affect people's lives, it touches on rights, opportunities, status and freedom." Alongside him was Anthropic cofounder and interpretability team lead Christopher Olah, representing a partnership between the Catholic Church and one of the biggest players in AI.
The letter elicited a wide range of reactions from in and around the tech industry. Nearly everyone believed the document would be infl …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: On Monday, Pope Leo XIV unveiled an encyclical letter addressing the societal implications of artificial intelligence. The letter, titled Magnifica Humanitas, warned that the “use of AI is never a purely technical matter: when it enters processes that affect people’s lives, it touches on rights, opportunities, status and freedom.” Alongside him was Anthropic cofounder and interpretability team lead Christopher Olah, representing a partnership between the Catholic Church and one of the biggest players in AI.The letter elicited a wide range of reactions from in and around the tech industry. Nearly everyone believed the document would be influential. Some critics questioned whether it went far enough, and others believed it should have discussed artificial general intelligence (AGI), which many companies insist is imminent. Still others thought the pope was spot-on.“It was a pretty clear subtweet of big tech CEOs who are out here blatantly declaring that they’re eliminating staff to replace ‘lower-value human capital’ with AI, and who are also buying their way into the political rooms where it happens in order to write the rules in their favor,” said Sacha Haworth, executive director of the Tech Oversight Project.The pope’s encyclical comes amid a backlash to AI’s growing power. Six in 10 US adults feel they have “little to no control” of how AI is used in their everyday lives, protests against the construction of data centers are increasingly common, and some people have even attempted attacks on AI CEOs themselves.RelatedThe race to AGI-pill the popePope Leo calls for being ‘profoundly human’ in the age of AIAs Olah’s appearance suggests, the pope’s missive describes AI as a technology that can have positive applications, and its tone earned mixed reactions. “I’m glad it’s critical of the AI companies though I think it should be more so,” Daniel Kokotajlo, an AI researcher and former OpenAI employee who is behind the nonprofit AI Futures Project, told The Verge. Conversely, Dr. Guru Sethupathy, GM of AI governance at software company Optro, was encouraged by indications that “Pope Leo and the Vatican are not against AI but rather how to pursue a responsible path that is best for humanity.”The decision to partner with the Vatican was a strategic move by Anthropic, a company that’s built its business on a carefully curated reputation of being a more trustworthy alternative than its competitors. Anthropic famously spent the last few months embroiled in a battle with the Pentagon over limits to military AI use, and a connection with another powerful institution could help bolster its status — and let it help shape future Vatican recommendations.One controversial aspect of the document, in many tech circles, was that it made no mention whatsoever of AGI or superintelligence; it allows that AI systems may “often surpass human intelligence in speed and computational capacity” but says they “lack the affective, relational and spiritual perspective through which human beings grow in wisdom.” Dean W. Ball, a senior fellow at the Foundation for American Innovation, wrote on X that the encyclical “would be much improved if it were less enamored of the traditional academia/civil society talking points on AI … and more engaged with where AI is headed. But instead of doing that, the encyclical dodges in the deepest sense, denying that AI ‘really thinks’ or ‘really learns.’” Kokotajlo also said he wished the letter took the possibility of AGI and superintelligence “more seriously.”But the document isn’t meant to do everything, several people in tech and in the Catholic world told The Verge.“It’s not about AI. It’s about protecting the human person in the age of AI.”“This is a major Catholic social teaching document,” Sister Susan Francois, assistant congregation leader of the Sisters of St. Joseph of Peace, said. “It’s not about AI. It’s about protecting the human person in the age of AI.” Brian Boyd, the US faith liaison for the Future of Life Institute and an instructor of Catholic social teaching at Notre Dame Seminary, called the document “more of a call to arms than a specific set of marching orders.” The pope called throughout the encyclical to “establish adequate regulatory tools capable of upholding justice and curbing the distorting effects of technological power,” but he stopped short of endorsing specific proposals.The Tech Oversight Project’s Haworth said she views the encyclical as “setting the compass for the moral direction of the world, or at least of the Western world … You don’t have to be Catholic to see your own concerns and your own wishes and fears in this document.” She said she expects her elected leaders to take on specific concerns over AGI and do their part to hold powerful tech companies accountable, rather than for the pope’s document to be “all things for all people.” She also called it a “warning shot for leaders, for politicians, because what this document talked about is the creation of a sub-class.”For some, the encyclical’s lack of attention to AGI wasn’t a problem, since they took it as the pope focusing on the real-world impacts of AI on vulnerable communities as the technology exists today, rather than some of the potential risks ahead for humanity as a whole if and when labs hit the (largely unspecified) AGI milestone.Aaron Fulkerson, CEO of Opaque Systems, a startup that helps customers organize encrypted data, said the pope is “actually looking at the system and highlighting something that a lot of us in tech would recognize as a systemic risk to humanity. And I’m not even talking about AGI. I’m talking about our global economy.”Even before the current wave of AI, tech industry centralization could be dangerous. A problem at cybersecurity firm CrowdStrike sidelined banks, hospitals, and airlines around the world, and an outage at Amazon Web Services took down a large swath of the internet, from Reddit to Venmo. For Fulkerson, the risks to the global economy only grow as power is concentrated in the hands of a couple of AI labs.“The thing that I’ve seen in the news has been this positioning around power dynamics of the pope versus tech bros, but I think everybody’s missing the bigger story here, which is that he’s looking at a system that is intrinsically risky,” Fulkerson said. “We’re sleepwalking into a world in which one or two labs are the cognitive infrastructure of every industry on earth — that means humanity is far less resilient, not more capable.”In the encyclical, the pope compared AI to the Tower of Babel, a structure he describes as “supported by a uniformity that eliminated diversity and that chose homogenization over communion.”. The world must “avoid the ‘Babel syndrome,’” he wrote: “the idolatry of profit that sacrifices the weak, a uniformity that neutralizes differences, and the pretense that a single language — even a digital one — can translate everything, including the mystery of the person, into data and performance.” In his reckoning, AI became not just a new technology, but a Biblical struggle. “The risk of dehumanization,” he wrote, “is an ancient and ever-new temptation that today takes on a technical guise.” The weight of those statements, not the technical specifics, is likely to be its lasting impact.
history.author: Hayden Field
history.descriptionZh:
周一，教皇利奥十四世发布了一封关于人工智能社会影响的通谕《Magnifica Humanitas》。该文件警告称，“人工智能的使用从来不是一个纯粹的技术问题：当它进入影响人们生活的过程时，就触及了权利、机会、地位和自由。”与他一同出席发布仪式的是Anthropic联合创始人兼可解释性团队负责人克里斯托弗·奥拉，这标志着天主教会与人工智能领域主要参与者之一的合作。

这封通谕引发了科技行业内外广泛的反应。几乎所有人都认为这份文件将具有影响力。一些批评者质疑其力度是否足够，另一些人则认为它本应讨论人工通用智能（AGI）——许多公司坚称AGI即将到来。还有一些人认为教皇的观点一针见血。

“这显然是在影射那些公开宣称要裁减员工、用人工智能取代‘低价值人力资本’，并试图通过政治关系为自己谋利的大型科技公司CEO们，”科技监督项目执行总监萨莎·霍沃斯表示。

教皇发布通谕之际，正值人工智能日益强大的反弹浪潮。六成美国成年人认为自己对人工智能在日常生活中的使用“几乎没有控制权”，针对数据中心建设的抗议越来越常见，甚至有人试图袭击人工智能公司的CEO。

正如奥拉的亮相所暗示的，教皇的这份文件将人工智能描述为一种可以产生积极应用的技术，其语气引发了截然不同的反应。“我很高兴它对人工智能公司持批评态度，但我认为批评得还不够严厉，”人工智能研究员、前OpenAI员工丹尼尔·科科塔伊洛告诉The Verge，他是非营利组织AI Futures Project的负责人。相反，软件公司Optro的人工智能治理总经理古鲁·塞图帕西博士则对“教皇利奥和梵蒂冈并不反对人工智能，而是寻求一条对人类最有利的负责任道路”这一表态感到鼓舞。

与梵蒂冈合作是Anthropic的战略性举措。这家公司建立在对自身声誉的精心维护之上，将自己定位为比竞争对手更值得信赖的替代方案。众所周知，Anthropic在过去几个月里一直与五角大楼就军事人工智能使用限制问题进行斗争，而与另一个强大机构的联系能够提升其地位，并有助于塑造未来梵蒂冈的指导意见。

在科技界，这份文件的一个争议点在于它完全没有提及AGI或超级智能。文件承认人工智能系统“在速度和计算能力上往往超越人类智能”，但认为它们“缺乏人类智慧成长所需的情感、关系和灵性视角。”美国创新基金会高级研究员迪恩·W·鲍尔在X上写道，这份通谕“如果能少一些对传统学术界/公民社会关于人工智能话题的陈词滥调的迷恋，更多地关注人工智能的发展方向，那么会更好……但相反，通谕在深层次上回避了问题，否认人工智能‘真正思考’或‘真正学习’。”科科塔伊洛也表示，他希望通谕能“更认真地”对待AGI和超级智能的可能性。

但几位科技界和天主教界人士告诉The Verge，这份文件的目的不是面面俱到。“它不是为了讨论人工智能，而是为了在人工智能时代保护人类个体。”

“这是一份重要的天主教社会训导文件，”圣约瑟夫和平修女会助理修女会长苏珊·弗朗索瓦修女说。“它不是关于人工智能，而是关于在人工智能时代保护人类个体。”未来生命研究所的美国信仰联络官、在圣母神学院教授天主教社会训导的布莱恩·博伊德称这份文件“更像是一份战斗号召，而不是一套具体的行动指令。”教皇在通谕中反复呼吁“建立足够的监管工具，以维护正义并遏制技术权力的扭曲效应”，但他没有支持具体的提案。

科技监督项目的霍沃斯表示，她将通谕视为“为世界的道德方向，至少是西方世界的道德方向设定了指南针……你不必是天主教徒，也能在这份文件中看到自己的关切、希望和恐惧。”她希望自己国家的民选领导人能够优先处理AGI的具体问题，并尽到自己的责任让强大的科技公司负责，而不是指望教皇的文件“对所有人来说都面面俱到”。她还称其为“对领导人、对政界人士的一记警告，因为这份文件讨论的是一个次等阶级的创造。”

对一些人来说，通谕对AGI缺乏关注并不是问题，因为他们认为教皇关注的是人工智能在当今技术状态下对弱势社区的实际影响，而不是实验室达到（尚未明确的）AGI里程碑时整个人类可能面临的潜在风险。

Opaque Systems（一家帮助客户组织加密数据的初创公司）CEO亚伦·富尔克森表示，教皇“实际上是在审视这个系统，并强调我们许多科技从业者都会认识到的人类系统性风险。我甚至不是在谈论AGI，我说的是我们的全球经济。”即使在当前人工智能浪潮之前，科技行业的集中化就可能带来危险。网络安全公司CrowdStrike的一次故障导致全球的银行、医院和航空公司瘫痪，亚马逊云服务（AWS）的一次宕机导致从Reddit到Venmo的大量互联网服务中断。对富尔克森来说，随着权力集中在少数几个人工智能实验室手中，全球经济风险只会越来越大。

“我在新闻中看到的是围绕‘教皇与科技大佬’的权力动态的站位，但我认为每个人都忽略了更大的故事：他在审视一个本质上具有风险的系统，”富尔克森说。“我们正梦游般走进一个世界，其中一两个实验室将成为地球上每个行业的认知基础设施——这意味着人类将变得远没有那么具有韧性，而不是变得更强大。”

在通谕中，教皇将人工智能比作巴别塔，他描述这座塔“由消除多样性的统一性支撑，选择同质化而非团结”。他写道，世界必须“避免‘巴别塔综合征’：牺牲弱者的利润崇拜、中和差异的统一性，以及假装单一语言——甚至数字语言——能够将一切，包括人的奥秘，转化为数据和表现。”在他的理解中，人工智能不仅成为一项新技术，更成为一场圣经式的斗争。“非人化的风险是一种古老而常新的诱惑，今天它披上了技术的外衣。”这些陈述的分量，而非技术细节，很可能才是其持久的影响所在。

history.title: SpaceX admits it can't find enough chips for orbital AI yet, requires 'significantly more than are currently available to us' — firm's risk factors in IPO paperwork also says ambitious TeraFab project may not be successful
history.description: SpaceX warns that it may not secure enough AI hardware to achieve its orbital AI ambitions even with supply from TeraFab. Also, Intel and Tesla may leave the TeraFab project.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Ahead of its hotly anticipated IPO, Elon Musk's SpaceX has admitted in its Form S-1 document that to fully scale its orbital AI ambitions, it needs more AI hardware than it can currently obtain. Furthermore, while the company says that the ambitious Terafab project may address chip constraints, it also noted that the project may not be successful and that current partners Tesla and Intel have no obligation to stick with the project long-term.As with all IPOs, the SEC requires that the company list all risk factors, both large and small, and these often even include 'Acts of God,' such as potential weather events, so the comments must be taken with the proper perspective. However, they do illustrate some of the roadblocks the company could perceive as genuine challenges to its business model.Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcript"Our ability to achieve orbital AI at scale depends on our ability to access a sufficient number of AI chips, significantly more than are currently available to us," the filing SpaceX reads. "Manufacturing and supply of servers and network equipment for our technical infrastructure, particularly for GPUs and other specialized components, is limited to a small number of qualified suppliers. We do not have any long-term or other material contractual arrangements with our direct chip suppliers; instead procuring all of our GPUs on a purchase-order basis."Latest Videos From You may like SpaceX says it is going to begin manufacturing GPUs Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project Elon Musk says TeraFab will use Intel's 14A process technology to make AI chips The current business arrangements with GPU suppliers — such as AMD, Nvidia, or manufacturing partners TSMC and Samsung Foundry — leave SpaceX and its xAI division exposed to 'fab capacity shortages, raw material constraints, geopolitical disruptions, and natural disasters affecting semiconductor manufacturing regions.'TSMC, the world's largest foundry and the world's largest maker of advanced logic chips, can barely meet demand for AI processors, and industry insiders warn that they are supply-constrained. For example, Nvidia increased its total supply, inclusive of inventory, purchase commitments, and prepaids, to $145 billion to ensure the supply of chips and other components. Other companies tend to do the same.To at least partly reduce its risks, Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI intend to build TeraFab, a dedicated semiconductor production facility that will exclusively produce chips for these three companies. For now, all we know about TeraFab is that it plans to operate a fab that will be located in a SpaceX campus in Texas and use Intel's 14A process technology to make chips. Elon Musk intends to invest tens of billions in TeraFab, but this does not guarantee that it will be a success, the S-1 warns."While we expect to construct Terafab to address such supply constraints, Terafab may not be successful, in which case we may not have other sources of sufficient AI chips to meet our orbital AI compute demands," another claim by SpaceX reads. "While Terafab is intended to expand our internal chip manufacturing capabilities and alleviate potential future AI chip shortages at SpaceX, particularly as we pursue orbital AI at scale, we expect to continue sourcing a significant portion of our compute hardware from third-party suppliers, and there can be no assurance that we will be able to achieve our objectives with respect to Terafab within the expected timeframes, or at all."Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsInterestingly, Intel and Tesla may leave the project, leaving TeraFab without a significant customer and process technology developer, which might ruin the whole project. "While we have a framework agreement with Tesla, neither Tesla nor Intel are obligated to remain a part of the project, and we may not enter into any such definitive agreements," the Form S-1 reads.
