history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: SpaceX承认目前无法为轨道AI找到足够芯片，需要的芯片数量“远超我们现有供应”——该公司IPO文件中的风险因素还指出雄心勃勃的TeraFab项目可能不会成功
history.descriptionZh:
在备受期待的IPO（首次公开募股）前夕，SpaceX在向美国证券交易委员会（SEC）提交的S-1文件中坦诚面临一个巨大挑战：要实现其雄心勃勃的“轨道级AI”规模化部署，公司需要的AI硬件远超当前所能获得的。尽管公司声称“Terafab”大型半导体制造项目可能解决芯片供应瓶颈，但文件也明确指出该项目存在失败风险，且当前合作伙伴特斯拉和英特尔并无长期留任该项目的义务。

SpaceX的S-1文件中写道：“我们能否实现规模化轨道AI，取决于能否获得足够数量的AI芯片，这远高于目前可用的数量。构建技术基础设施所需的服务器和网络设备，特别是GPU及其他专用组件的制造与供应，目前仅局限于少数几家合格供应商。我们与直接芯片供应商之间没有任何长期或实质性的合同安排，所有GPU均通过采购订单方式获取。”这番表述揭示了公司在核心算力供应上的脆弱性。

所谓“轨道级AI”，是SpaceX为其星链卫星网络部署人工智能计算能力的核心战略设想。通过在低轨道卫星上集成AI硬件，可以实现实时数据处理、自主导航、网络优化等功能，极大提升星链的智能化水平和响应速度。然而，这一愿景的实现严重依赖于先进AI芯片——尤其是高算力GPU的充足供应。目前，全球AI芯片市场高度集中，主要供应商包括AMD、英伟达、台积电和三星代工厂等。SpaceX在文件中承认，现有采购模式使其暴露在“代工厂产能短缺、原材料限制、地缘政治动荡以及影响半导体制造区域的自然灾害”等多重风险之下。

行业背景进一步加剧了这一困境。作为全球最大晶圆代工厂及先进逻辑芯片制造商的台积电，目前产能几乎被AI处理器订单填满，业界普遍警示其供应严重受限。以英伟达为例，为保障芯片及其他组件供应，该公司已将总供应量——包括库存、采购承诺及预付款——提升至1450亿美元。这一趋势下，SpaceX面临的市场竞争极为激烈。

为部分化解这一风险，SpaceX联合特斯拉及xAI（马斯克的AI公司）计划共建“Terafab”——一座专门为这三家公司生产芯片的专属半导体制造厂。根据现有信息，Terafab将坐落于SpaceX在德克萨斯州的园区内，并采用英特尔的14A工艺技术进行芯片制造。马斯克计划向该工厂投入数百亿美元。然而，S-1文件冷静地警告：“我们虽预计建造Terafab来解决供应限制，但Terafab可能不会成功。若如此，我们可能没有其他足够的AI芯片来源满足轨道级AI计算需求。”这表明，即便是如此庞大的投资也不能确保技术成功。

更关键的是，Terafab项目还存在合作者退出的风险。文件中指出：“尽管我们与特斯拉有框架协议，但特斯拉和英特尔均无义务继续参与该项目，我们可能无法签署任何最终协议。”这意味着，若英特尔放弃工艺技术提供、或特斯拉停止作为客户，Terafab将失去存在的两大支柱，项目可能就此瓦解。

从技术影响与应用场景来看，Terafab一旦成功，将彻底改变SpaceX、特斯拉和xAI的算力供给方式。它为这三家公司打造了专属、定制化的AI芯片生产线，能根据卫星通信、自动驾驶和通用AI等垂直领域需求优化芯片架构，极大提升效率、降低成本，并避免对外部供应链的依赖。然而，如果项目失败，SpaceX将继续在全球芯片短缺的浪潮中艰难争夺GPU资源，其“轨道级AI”战略也将面临严重制约。

总体而言，SpaceX这份S-1文件揭示了尖端AI硬件市场供需极度失衡的现实，以及企业自建芯片产能所面临的巨大风险与不确定性。它既是一份面向投资者的风险披露，也是AI产业链日益紧张、技术自主与控制能力成为企业核心竞争力的真实写照。随IPO推进，市场将密切关注Terafab进展及其对SpaceX业务能否按期开展的关键影响。

history.title: Taiwan authorities arrest three on suspicion of smuggling Nvidia chips to China — operation allegedly used Japan as transshipment point before forwarding banned Supermicro servers to Hong Kong
history.description: Reports say that the three individuals successfully shipped a batch of banned Nvidia AI chips in Super Micro servers to China from Taiwan to Hong Kong via Japan using falsified documentation.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Prosecutors from the Taiwan Keelung District Office arrested three individuals last week, suspected of smuggling Nvidia chips to China. According to Bloomberg, the Taiwanese authorities also seized 50 Super Micro servers with falsified documents that were reportedly ultimately destined for Hong Kong via Japan. Aside from the seized servers, authorities say that an earlier batch of shipments had already been smuggled successfully using fake documentation.This is the first arrest Taiwan has made in connection with the U.S. crackdown on AI chip smuggling, and the first to reveal Japan as a transshipment point for these illicit goods. Many smuggling operations have been using Southeast Asian nations to route Nvidia chips to China. However, countries like Singapore and Malaysia have started cracking down on these outfits, meaning smugglers need to look for other ways to get the banned hardware under the radar.It is surprising that the recently broken-up operation in Taiwan routed the servers to Japan, especially as the country is known for its strict customs enforcement. The latter is a staunch U.S. ally and is one of the key pillars of the United States’ strategy in the Pacific to contain China. However, some Chinese AI companies also use the country to rent Nvidia AI chips owned by foreign firms that they otherwise wouldn’t be able to access domestically — a route that’s currently allowed by Washington’s export control rules. Japanese authorities from the Ministry of Finance's Customs Bureau and the Ministry of Economy, Trade, and Industry have remained silent on the issue, and they also did not confirm if they’ve been in touch with their Taiwanese counterparts regarding the arrest.Latest Videos From You may like Taiwan raids 12 locations in its first formal crackdown on Nvidia AI chip smuggling After $2.5 billion Supermicro smuggling bust, Nvidia CEO urges company to fix export control compliance Supermicro employees accused of smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia hardware to China While the arrested individuals were illegally dealing Supermicro servers powered by Nvidia chips, the Taiwanese authorities have refrained from accusing both companies of breaking U.S. export control laws. Nevertheless, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said that the company explains the rules and regulations to its partners and is urging the company to fix its compliance systems. “Ultimately Super Micro has to run their own company,” Huang said to reporters. “I hope that they will enhance and improve their regulation compliance and avoid that from happening in the future.”It’s unclear which chips were being smuggled, but the last-generation Nvidia H200 have already received clearance from the White House for export to China. However, Chinese customs officials were instructed to block these chips at the border, with the order recently being expanded to RTX 5090D V2 gaming GPUs. history.titleZh: 台湾当局逮捕三名涉嫌向中国走私英伟达芯片人员——据称利用日本作为转运点，再将违禁超微服务器运往香港
history.descriptionZh:
台湾基隆地检署上周逮捕了三名涉嫌走私英伟达（Nvidia）AI芯片至中国的嫌疑人，并查获了50台搭载英伟达芯片的超微（Super Micro）服务器，这些服务器均使用伪造文件，原计划经日本运往香港。据调查，此前已有一批货物通过类似手段成功走私出境。这是台湾首次因美国对华AI芯片出口管制而实施的正式逮捕行动，也是首次披露日本成为此类非法货物的转运站。

过去，许多走私团伙利用东南亚国家（如新加坡、马来西亚）作为中转站，将英伟达芯片运往中国。但随着这些国家开始加强打击，走私者不得不寻找新的路线。此次台湾破获的案件中，走私团伙选择经日本转运，令人感到意外——日本不仅是美国的坚定盟友，也是美国太平洋遏制中国战略的关键支柱，且以严格的海关执法著称。不过，一些中国AI公司确实通过在日本租用外资企业拥有的英伟达AI芯片来间接获取这些硬件，这种做法目前尚未被美国出口管制规则明确禁止。日本财务省关税局和经济产业省对此事保持沉默，也未确认是否已与台湾相关部门就逮捕事件进行沟通。

被捕嫌疑人涉嫌非法交易搭载英伟达芯片的超微服务器，但台湾当局并未指控英伟达或超微违反美国出口管制法律。英伟达CEO黄仁勋表示，公司已向合作伙伴解释了相关法规，并敦促超微加强合规体系。“最终超微必须自己管理好公司，”黄仁勋对记者说，“我希望他们能加强和改进合规监管，避免此类事件再次发生。”

目前尚不清楚走私的具体是哪些芯片，但上一代英伟达H200芯片已获得白宫对华出口许可，然而中国海关被指示禁止这些芯片入境，近期该禁令范围还扩大至RTX 5090D V2游戏显卡。这意味着即使是获得美国出口许可的硬件，在中国边境也会被拦截。

从技术原理看，英伟达H200是专为AI训练和推理设计的高性能GPU加速卡，其核心创新在于采用HBM3e高带宽内存，显存容量和带宽较前代H100大幅提升，能够显著加速大语言模型（如GPT-4）的训练和推理效率。而RTX 5090D V2则是面向消费级市场的游戏显卡，但经过定制以降低AI算力，仍被中国海关列为限制品。超微服务器作为整合这些芯片的硬件平台，在AI数据中心中扮演关键角色。

性能参数方面，H200的HBM3e内存容量达到141GB，带宽达4.8TB/s，较H100的80GB/3.35TB/s提升明显。在测试中，H200处理大型语言模型时的推理吞吐量可提升近2倍。对比之下，RTX 5090D V2的AI算力被限制在约200 TOPS（INT8），远低于常规版本的500+ TOPS，但依然具有相当的计算能力。

这一事件凸显了美国出口管制在AI芯片领域的深远影响。英伟达芯片（尤其是H200、B200等高端型号）是中国AI公司发展大模型、自动驾驶、智慧城市等应用不可或缺的核心算力。面对封锁，中国公司一方面通过租赁海外算力（如日本数据中心）、购买降级版芯片（如H20）等方式应对，另一方面加速国产AI芯片（如华为昇腾910B）的替代进程。然而，走私活动的存在说明市场对受限芯片的需求依然旺盛，而台湾作为全球半导体供应链的关键节点，此次逮捕可能促使更多国家和地区加强执法协作。

history.title: NVIDIA Vera CPU Is ‘Packing a Heavy-Hitting Punch’ Against Competition
history.description: The shift to agentic AI creates a new CPU requirement for the AI factory: fast cores, massive memory bandwidth and the ability to sustain high performance when all cores are active. Initial benchmark results published by Phoronix today show that the NVIDIA Vera CPU meets this need. For this first public look, the benchmark scope […]
history.content: The shift to agentic AI creates a new CPU requirement for the AI factory: fast cores, massive memory bandwidth and the ability to sustain high performance when all cores are active. Initial benchmark results published by Phoronix today show that the NVIDIA Vera CPU meets this need. For this first public look, the benchmark scope was centered on the agentic workloads Vera was designed for in the modern data center. The Vera CPU delivers the throughput AI factories need while optimizing platform power. Eighty-eight NVIDIA custom Olympus cores, 1.2TB/s of memory bandwidth and a high-speed, on-chip fabric results in a CPU platform that combines core performance and memory bandwidth in an efficient power envelope. NVIDIA Olympus Delivers Aggressive Performance At the heart of Vera are custom NVIDIA Olympus CPU cores. Fully compatible with the Armv9.2 instruction set architecture, Olympus is designed for the sequential CPU work underpinning agentic AI: branch-heavy runtimes, sandboxed code, data processing and orchestration. Vera’s monolithic die, wide cores, advanced branch prediction and the second-generation NVIDIA Scalable Coherency Fabric help Vera keep data moving across all 88 cores. Phoronix’s testing of a single-socket Vera CPU — rated at 450-watt thermal design power with less than 30 watts of memory power — showed that it delivers outstanding performance within that power profile, along with generational gains across a broad array of workloads spanning code compilation, file compression, video transcoding, Python, Java and database management. These are the same kinds of CPU-heavy tasks that agents and AI factories run every day: compiling code, executing runtimes, compressing data, querying databases and coordinating large software stacks. “Going into this, I didn’t really know what to expect of NVIDIA’s Vera with the new Olympus cores,” wrote Michael Larabel, founder and principal author of Phoronix. “But in the end I was left realizing this is the most formidable competition to Intel and AMD x86_64 processors ever realized.” ‘Incredible Advantage’ in Memory Performance Agentic workloads are not limited by core count alone. They need high core utilization and sustained memory bandwidth, making memory performance per watt a critical part of overall CPU efficiency. Vera incorporates a second-generation LPDDR5X memory subsystem, enabling dramatically lower energy per bit compared to DDR5. This allows Vera to offer up to a massive 1.2 TB/s of bandwidth — up to 2x the peak memory bandwidth compared with traditional CPUs in less than 30 watts of memory power, as opposed to more than 100 watts for traditional DDR5. In Phoronix STREAM TRIAD testing, Vera sustained 90% of its peak memory bandwidth — achieving the highest percentage of rated peak bandwidth of any CPU tested by Phoronix — and delivered over 4x the memory bandwidth per core compared with traditional x86 CPUs. “NVIDIA Vera with its LPDDR5X memory was showing its incredible advantage in memory performance over current Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC processors,” Larabel wrote. However, peak bandwidth is only part of the story. AI factory workloads run many sandboxes, tool calls and data services at the same time. In separate testing with Vera, Prime Intellect found that Vera maintained high bandwidth and low, consistent memory latency as more workloads ran in parallel — the kind of predictable performance needed for agentic AI. A Large Generational Leap — and Leadership in Phoronix Testing Compared with the prior-generation NVIDIA Grace CPU, Vera delivered a 1.6x geometric mean increase in Phoronix’s testing — an incredible generation-over-generation gain. “The difference from Grace to Vera was consistently exceeding my expectations for gen-on-gen performance we typically see for processors,” Larabel wrote. “NVIDIA’s Vera CPU with its in-house-designed Olympus CPU cores ends up packing a heavy-hitting punch with competitiveness to Intel/AMD x86_64 CPUs that I have never seen out of any other ARM or non-x86_64 processors.” Vera led the tested CPU field, delivering a 1.5x overall performance advantage compared with a latest-generation 128-core x86 processor. The gains showed up in practical developer workloads. Single-socket Vera compiled a default Linux kernel in just 20 seconds, the fastest result Phoronix measured in that test. Vera delivered 2x faster Linux kernel compilation on a per-core basis compared with a 128-core processor. “On a [geometric] mean basis, the NVIDIA Vera delivered 10% better performance than the AMD EPYC 9575F 5.0 GHz high frequency processor,” Larabel wrote. Vera in Customer Testing, Coming Soon From Partners At NVIDIA GTC, NVIDIA announced widespread ecosystem support for Vera, spanning AI natives, supercomputing centers, cloud service providers and infrastructure providers. NVIDIA has also delivered the first Vera CPUs to leading AI companies and cloud providers, marking an important milestone as Vera moves toward partner availability in the second half of the year. Vera will be available from partners in dual- and single-socket systems, with air-cooled and liquid-cooled options to support AI factory deployments, from standard enterprise data centers to high-density agentic AI infrastructure. Learn more about NVIDIA Vera.
history.author: Diana Aung
history.titleZh: NVIDIA Vera CPU 对竞争对手“重拳出击”
history.descriptionZh:
随着人工智能向智能体（agentic AI）方向演进，AI工厂对CPU提出了全新的要求：需要快速的核心、海量的内存带宽，以及在全核满载时依然能维持高性能的能力。Phoronix 今日发布的初步基准测试结果显示，NVIDIA Vera CPU 恰好满足了这一需求。作为首次公开亮相，本次测试的基准范围聚焦于Vera在现代数据中心中为智能体工作负载设计的目标场景。Vera CPU 在提供AI工厂所需的吞吐量的同时，优化了平台功耗。凭借88个NVIDIA自研Olympus核心、1.2TB/s的内存带宽以及高速片上互连结构，Vera在高效功耗包络内实现了核心性能与内存带宽的完美结合。

## NVIDIA Olympus 核心带来强劲性能

Vera的核心是NVIDIA自研的Olympus CPU核心，完全兼容Armv9.2指令集架构。Olympus专为支撑智能体AI的顺序性CPU工作而设计，包括分支密集型运行时、沙盒代码、数据处理和编排调度。Vera采用单芯片设计，拥有宽核心、先进的分支预测以及第二代NVIDIA可扩展一致性互连（Scalable Coherency Fabric），帮助Vera在全部88个核心间保持数据流畅运转。Phoronix 对单插槽Vera CPU（额定热设计功耗450瓦，内存功耗低于30瓦）的测试显示，它在该功耗范围内提供了卓越的性能，并在代码编译、文件压缩、视频转码、Python、Java和数据库管理等广泛的工作负载上实现了跨代增长。这些正是智能体和AI工厂每天运行的CPU密集型任务：编译代码、执行运行时、压缩数据、查询数据库以及协调大型软件栈。

Phoronix 创始人兼首席作者 Michael Larabel 评价道：“说实话，我原本对NVIDIA Vera搭配新Olympus核心的表现没什么预期。但最终结果让我意识到，这是迄今为止对Intel和AMD x86_64处理器最有力的挑战。”

## 内存性能的“惊人优势”

智能体工作负载的瓶颈不仅仅是核心数量，它们还需要高核心利用率以及持续的内存带宽，这使得每瓦内存性能成为CPU整体效率的关键部分。Vera集成了第二代LPDDR5X内存子系统，相比DDR5，每比特能耗显著降低。这使得Vera能够提供高达1.2TB/s的带宽——相比传统CPU的峰值内存带宽提升可达2倍，而内存功耗不到30瓦（传统DDR5通常超过100瓦）。在Phoronix的STREAM TRIAD测试中，Vera保持了峰值带宽的90%，这是Phoronix测试过的所有CPU中达到的额定峰值带宽百分比最高的一次，同时每核心内存带宽达到了传统x86 CPU的4倍以上。Larabel写道：“NVIDIA Vera凭借其LPDDR5X内存，在内存性能上对当前Intel Xeon和AMD EPYC处理器展现了惊人的优势。”

但峰值带宽只是故事的一部分。AI工厂工作负载会同时运行大量沙盒、工具调用和数据服务。在另一项与Vera的独立测试中，Prime Intellect 发现，随着并行工作负载的增加，Vera依然能保持高带宽和低而一致的内存延迟——这正是智能体AI所需的可预测性能。

## 巨大的代际飞跃——Phoronix测试中的领先地位

与上一代NVIDIA Grace CPU相比，Vera在Phoronix测试中的几何平均性能提升了1.6倍——这是令人难以置信的代际增长。Larabel写道：“从Grace到Vera的差异，持续超出了我对处理器典型代际性能提升的预期。NVIDIA Vera CPU搭载其自研Olympus核心，对Intel/AMD x86_64 CPU的竞争力，是我从未在任何其他ARM或非x86_64处理器上见过的。”

Vera在所有测试CPU中领先，与最新一代128核x86处理器相比，整体性能优势达到1.5倍。这些提升体现在实际的开发者工作负载中：单插槽Vera仅用20秒就完成了默认Linux内核的编译，这是Phoronix在该测试中测得的最快结果。在每核心基础上，Vera的Linux内核编译速度是128核处理器的2倍。Larabel指出：“在几何平均基础上，NVIDIA Vera的性能比AMD EPYC 9575F 5.0 GHz高频处理器高出10%。”

## 客户测试中——合作伙伴即将推出

在NVIDIA GTC大会上，NVIDIA宣布了Vera获得广泛生态系统支持，涵盖AI原生企业、超算中心、云服务提供商和基础设施提供商。NVIDIA已向领先的AI公司和云提供商交付了首批Vera CPU，这标志着Vera向今年下半年的合作伙伴供货迈出了重要一步。Vera将提供双插槽和单插槽系统，并支持风冷和液冷选项，以满足从标准企业数据中心到高密度智能体AI基础设施的AI工厂部署需求。更多信息请关注NVIDIA Vera。
